blocks:
Sūrat Ṭā Hā

Meccan, and it is 135 verses

(١) طه ** (1) Ṭā Hā. Its interpretation has preceded in Sūrat al-Baqarah. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for طه ** (Ṭā Hā), it is a name among the names of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), and its meaning is: O Seeker of the Truth, the Guide to Him.

(٢) ما أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ لِتَشْقى ** (2) We did not send down the Qur’ān to you that you should be miserable, He said: Rather, that you should be happy (لتسعد). And al-Qummi narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) that he said: When the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) prayed, he would stand upon the tips of his feet until they swelled. So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, sent down طه ** (Ṭā Hā), which in the language of (طيّ) means: O Muhammad, ما أَنْزَلْنا ** (We did not send down...) the Ayah. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) was with 'A'ishah on her night, and she said: "O Messenger of Allah, why do you exhaust yourself when Allah has forgiven you what preceded of your sin and what is yet to come?" He replied: "O 'A'ishah, should I not be a grateful servant (عَبداً شكوراً)?" He said: And the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) used to stand upon the tips of his feet, so Allah, the Glorified, sent down طه ما أَنْزَلْنا ** (Ṭā Hā, We did not send down...) the Ayah. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Kazim, from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) stood for ten years upon the tips of his toes until his feet swelled and his face turned yellow, standing the entire night, until He was admonished concerning that. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: طه ما أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ لِتَشْقى ** (Ṭā Hā, We did not send down the Qur’ān to you that you should be miserable), rather, that you should be happy (لتسعد) through it. It is said that al-shaqā' (الشقاء) is common in the meaning of exhaustion (التّعب). And from this usage is the saying: "More exhausted (أشقى) than the trainer of a colt," and "The master of the people is the most exhausting (أشقاهم) of them." Perhaps He turned to this meaning (الشقاء) to indicate that it (the Qur'an) was sent down to him so that he might be happy (ليسعد).

(٣) إِلَّا تَذْكِرَةً ** but as an admonition لِمَنْ يَخْشى ** for him in whose heart there is fear (خشية) and tenderness (رقّة), who is affected by the warning (الإنذار). (٤) تَنْزِيلاً مِمَّنْ خَلَقَ الْأَرْضَ وَالسَّماواتِ الْعُلى ** (A sending down from Him who created the earth and the high heavens) It is the plural of al-ʿUlyā (العُليا), the feminine of al-Aʿlā (الأعلى). The greatness of the status of the revelation (المنزل) is due to its relation to the One Whose attributes (صفاته) and actions (أفعاله) these are. (٥) الرَّحْمنُ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ اسْتَوى (The All-beneficent, settled on the Throne.) In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He says: He encompassed the dominion (الملك). And the complete exegesis of this has preceded in the verse concerning the sorcerers (السّحرة) from Surat al-A'raf (سورة الأعراف). (٦) لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما وَما تَحْتَ الثَّرى (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, and whatever is between them, and whatever is beneath the subsoil.) In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He recited this verse and then said: Everything is upon the subsoil (الثّرى), and the subsoil (الثّرى) is upon the Power (القدرة), and the Power (القدرة) carries everything. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The earth is upon the fish (الحوت), and the fish (الحوت) is upon the water (الماء), and the water (الماء) is upon the rock (الصّخرة), and the rock (الصّخرة) is upon the horn of a smooth bull (ثور أملس), and the bull (الثور) is upon the subsoil (الثرى). And at that point, the knowledge of the scholars (العلماء) goes astray. It is said: He began by creating the earth and the heavens, which are the foundations (أصول) of the universe. He prioritized the earth because it is closer to the senses (الحسّ) and more apparent to them than the heavens. Then He pointed to the manner of bringing the creatures (الكائنات) into existence and managing their affairs, by intending the Throne (العرش), from which He executed the decrees (الأحكام) and the destinies (التقادير), and from which He sent down the means (الأسباب) in an arrangement and measures as necessitated by His Wisdom (حكمته) and as related to His Will (مشيّته), so that He might thereby demonstrate the perfection of His Power (قدرته) and His Will (ارادته). And since Power (القدرة) is subordinate to Will (الارادة), and Will (الارادة) is inseparable from Knowledge (العلم), He followed that up by mentioning the encompassing nature of His Knowledge (علمه) over the manifest (جلّيات) and hidden (خفيّاتها) matters equally, and thus He said:

(٧) وَإِنْ تَجْهَرْ بِالْقَوْلِ فَإِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ السِّرَّ وَأَخْفى (7) If you speak loudly, [it makes no difference, for] He indeed knows the secret and what is more hidden.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them both), regarding this verse: The secret (السرّ) is what you conceal within yourself, and what is more hidden (وأخفى) is what crossed your mind and then you forgot it.

(٨) اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ لَهُ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى (8) Allah—there is no god except Him—to Him belong the Best Names. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Verily, Allah the Almighty has ninety-nine Names; whoever enumerates them shall enter Paradise. And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The like of it.

(٩) وَهَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسى ** (9) And has the story of Moses come to you? It was said: He followed the preparation of his prophethood with the story of Moses so that he (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) might follow his example in bearing the burdens of prophethood, conveying the message, and being patient in enduring hardships, for this surah is among the first of what was revealed. (١٠) إِذْ رَأى ناراً ** (10) When he saw a fire, It was said that he asked Shu'ayb for permission to depart to his mother and he left with his family. When he reached the Valley of Tuwa, where al-Tur is located, a son was born to him (peace be upon him) on a cold, dark, snowy night, and it was the night of Friday. He had lost the way and his livestock had scattered, when he saw a fire from the side of al-Tur. So he said to his family: Stay (امْكُثُوا), meaning: Remain in your place. Indeed, I have perceived a fire, meaning: I saw it with a sighting in which there is no doubt. And it was said that al-īnas (الإيناس) is the sighting of that by which one is comforted (يُؤْنَسُ بِهِ). I may bring you a brand from it, with a flame of the fire, or find a guidance at the fire. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: I will bring you a brand of fire so that you may warm yourselves from the cold, or find a guidance at the fire. He had missed the way, meaning: or I will find a path near the fire.

**(١١) فَلَمَّا أَتاها ** (11) When he came to it i.e., the fire. It was said that he found a white fire burning brightly in a green tree.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: So he approached the fire seeking to take a brand, and behold, there was a tree and a fire blazing upon it. When he went towards the fire to take a brand from it, the fire swooped down towards him, so he was terrified and ran away. And the fire returned to the tree, so he turned back to it, and it had indeed returned to the tree. So he returned a second time to take a brand, and it swooped down towards him, so he ran away and left it. Then he turned back, and it had returned to the tree. So he returned to it a third time, and it swooped down towards him, so he ran away and did not follow up (لم يعقّب), meaning he did not return. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, called out to him. And the rest of the narration will come in Surat al-Qasas: نُودِيَ يا مُوسى (He was called, 'O Moses').

(١٢) إِنِّي أَنَا رَبُّكَ ** (12) I am indeed your Lord! وقرئ بفتح الهمزة فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوىً ** (So take off your sandals. You are indeed in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā.) And it was recited with the fathah on the hamzah (of inna). طوى (Ṭuwā) is an appositional explanation (عطف بيان) for the valley (الواد), for it was named by it. And it was recited with tanwin (as Ṭuwan). It was said that He commanded him to take off his sandals because being barefoot (الحفوة) is humility (تواضع) and proper conduct (ادب). In al-Faqih, al-Ikmal, and al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and al-Qummi said: He was only commanded to take them off because they were made from the hide of a dead donkey (جلد حمار ميّت). And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Hujjah al-Qa'im (peace be upon him), in a hadith: It was said to him: "Inform me, O son of the Messenger of Allah, concerning Allah's command to His Prophet Moses (peace be upon him): فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ ** (So take off your sandals. You are indeed in the sacred valley), for the jurists (فقهاء) of the two groups (الفريقين) claim that they were made from the hide of a carcass (إهاب الميتة)." He (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said: "Whoever says that has fabricated a lie against Moses and deemed him ignorant (استجهله) in his prophethood, because the matter concerning them cannot be devoid of two possibilities: either Moses' prayer while wearing them was permissible (جائزة) or it was not permissible (غير جائزة). If his prayer was permissible, then it was permissible for him to wear them in that spot, even if it were not sacred. And if it was sacred and purified (مقدّسة مطهّرة), it is not more sacred or purer than the prayer itself. And if his prayer was not permissible while wearing them, then this necessitates that Moses did not know the lawful from the unlawful (الحلال من الحرام) and did not know what prayer was permissible in and what was not permissible, and this is disbelief (كفر)." It was said: "And inform me, O my Master (مولاي), concerning the esoteric interpretation (التّأويل) of it." He (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said: "Moses (peace be upon him) was supplicating secretly (ناجى) to his Lord in the Sacred Valley, and he said: 'O Lord, I have purified my love for You and cleansed my heart from all besides You.' And he was intensely devoted to his family (أهله). So Allah the Almighty said: فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ ** (So take off your sandals), meaning: Remove the love of your family from your heart, if your love for Me is pure and your heart is cleansed from inclining toward anyone besides Me." And in al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means: Remove your two fears (خوفيك), meaning his fear of losing his family, whom he had left behind in labor (تمخض), and his fear of Pharaoh (فرعون). And in al-Ikmal, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): That which is similar in meaning. And in al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family): He was asked about بِالْوادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ ** (the sacred valley), and He said: "Because the souls (الأرواح) were sanctified (قدّست) therein, and the angels (الملائكة) were chosen (اصطفيت) therein." وَكَلَّمَ اللهُ ** the Almighty مُوسى تَكْلِيماً. (and Allah spoke to Moses directly.)

(13) وَأَنَا اخْتَرْتُكَ ** (And I have chosen you) I have selected you (اصْطَفيتك) for prophethood. And it was recited (قرئ): انّا اخترناك (Indeed, We have chosen you). فَاسْتَمِعْ لِما يُوحى ** (so listen to what is revealed [to you]) to that which is revealed to you, or to the revelation (الوحي). And the lām (اللام) potentially relates to both verbs. (14) إِنَّنِي أَنَا اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاعْبُدْنِي ** (Indeed, I am Allah. There is no god except Me. So worship Me) [This is] a substitute for what is revealed, indicating that it is restricted to establishing Monotheism (التوحيد), which is the ultimate goal of knowledge, and the command for worship (العبادة), which is the perfection of action. وَأَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ لِذِكْرِي ** (and maintain the prayer for My remembrance.) It was said: He specified it [the prayer] with mention and singled it out with the command due to the reason He attached to its establishment, which is the remembrance of the Worshipped One (المعبود) and the occupation of the heart and the tongue with His remembrance.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): If a prayer has passed you by (فاتتك صلاة) and you remember it during the time of another [prayer], then if you know that if you pray the one that passed you by, you will still be within the time of the other, then begin with the one that passed you by. For Allah says: أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ لِذِكْرِي (and maintain the prayer for My remembrance). The narrative (الحديث).

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), its meaning is: **أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ ** (and maintain the prayer) whenever you remember that a prayer is due upon you, whether you are within its time or not.

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever forgets a prayer, let him pray it when he remembers it. There is no expiation (كفّارة) for it other than that." And He recited: أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ لِذِكْرِي. (and maintain the prayer for My remembrance.)

And al-Qummi said: If you forget it, then remember it, pray it.

(15) إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ ** (15) Indeed, the Hour is coming, It is inevitable (كائنة لا محالة). أَكادُ أُخْفِيها ** I almost conceal it, It was said: meaning, I conceal its time. In al-Majma' and al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): أَكادُ أُخْفِيها ** from Myself (من نفسي), and indeed, it is thus in the recitation (قراءة) of Ubayy. And al-Qummi said: "From Myself (من نفسي), thus was it revealed." It was asked: "How does He conceal it from Himself?" He replied: "He made it without a fixed time (من غير وقت)." And it was said: Its meaning is, I almost reveal it (أكاد أظهرها), derived from (أخفاه) when He removes its concealment (إذا سلب خفاه). لِتُجْزى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِما تَسْعى ** so that every soul may be recompensed for what it strives for. [This phrase] is related to (آتِيَةٌ) (is coming) or to (أُخْفِيها) according to the latter meaning [of revealing].

(١٦) فَلا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنْها So do not let him bar you from it, referring to believing in the Hour (السّاعة) or the prayer (الصلاة), مَنْ لا يُؤْمِنُ بِها وَاتَّبَعَ هَواهُ فَتَرْدى who does not believe in it and follows his own desires, lest you perish, meaning: you will perish (فتهلك) by being barred (بالانصداد) or by his barring (بصدّه). (16) So do not let him who does not believe in it and follows his own desires bar you from it, lest you perish.

(١٧) وَما تِلْكَ بِيَمِينِكَ 'What is that in your right hand,' is an interrogation (استفهام) that implies an awakening (استيقاظاً) to the wonders (العجائب) He will show him in it. يا مُوسى (O Moses) is a repetition (تكرير) for the sake of increasing familiarity (الاستيناس) and alerting (التنبيه). (17) 'What is that in your right hand, Moses?'

(١٨) قالَ هِيَ عَصايَ أَتَوَكَّؤُا عَلَيْها He said, 'This is my staff. I lean on it,' meaning: I rely upon it (أعتمد عليها) when I am tired (عييت) or when I stand at the head of the flock (القطيع). وَأَهُشُّ بِها عَلى غَنَمِي and with it I beat down leaves for my sheep, meaning: I strike the leaves (الورق) with it upon the heads of my sheep (غنمي). وَلِيَ فِيها مَآرِبُ أُخْرى (and I have other uses for it) meaning: other needs (حاجات أخر), such as that when he traveled (سار), he would place it upon his shoulder (عاتقه) and hang his tools (أدواته) upon it. And when he was in the wilderness (البرّية), he would plant it and place the two fire-sticks (الزندين) upon its two forks (شعبتيها), and throw the cloak (الكساء) over it and seek shade (أستظلّ) with it. And if the rope (الرشا) was short, he would extend it with the staff. And if predators (السّباع) approached his sheep, he would fight them with it. (18) He said, 'This is my staff. I lean on it, and with it I beat down leaves for my sheep, and I have other uses for it.'

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Due to the fear (الفرق), he was unable to speak, so he gathered his words and said: فِيها مَآرِبُ أُخْرى ** (and I have other uses for it), meaning: other needs (حوائج اخرى).

(١٩) قالَ أَلْقِها يا مُوسى (19) He said, 'Cast it down, O Moses!' (٢٠) فَأَلْقاها فَإِذا هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعى (20) So he cast it down, and behold, it was a serpent, moving swiftly. (٢١) قالَ خُذْها وَلا تَخَفْ (21) He said, 'Take hold of it, and do not fear. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): So Moses (peace be upon him) was terrified of it and ran away, whereupon Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, called out to him: خُذْها وَلا تَخَفْ (Take hold of it, and do not fear). سَنُعِيدُها سِيرَتَهَا الْأُولى ** We will restore it to its former state.' [Meaning] its previous form (هيئتها) and state (حالتها). السير (the manner/way) is used metaphorically (تجوز بها) for the way (الطّريقة) and the form (الهيئة).

(٢٢) وَاضْمُمْ يَدَكَ إِلى جَناحِكَ ** (22) And put your hand to your armpit (under the upper arm) تَخْرُجْ بَيْضاءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوءٍ ** it will come out white, without any blemish (without any defect (عاهة); He alluded by it to leprosy (البرص)).

In Tibb al-A'immah, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means without leprosy (برص).

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That is, without any ailment (علّة). That is because Moses (peace be upon him) was intensely dark-skinned (شديد السّمرة), so when he brought his hand out of his garment opening, the world was illuminated for him.

(٢٣) آيَةً أُخْرى (23) another sign: a second miracle (معجزة ثانية). (٢٤) لِنُرِيَكَ مِنْ آياتِنَا الْكُبْرى (24) that We may show you some of Our major signs. (٢٥) اذْهَبْ إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ (25) Go to Pharaoh: with these two signs, and invite him to worship (العبادة). إِنَّهُ طَغى (25) he has indeed rebelled: he disobeyed and became arrogant (عصى وتكبّر). (٢٦) قالَ رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي (26) He said, 'My Lord! Open my breast for me. (٢٧) وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي (27) and make my affair easy for me. When Allah commanded him concerning a great matter (خطب عظيم), he asked Him to open his breast and expand his heart so that he might bear its burdens and endure its hardships. (٢٨) وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِنْ لِسانِي (28) and loosen the knot from my tongue, يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي (28) that they may understand my speech. It is said that there was a lisp (رتّة) in his tongue due to an ember (جمرة) that he had put into his mouth.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Pharaoh used to kill the children of the Children of Israel whenever they were born, yet he raised Musa and honored him, not knowing that his destruction would be at his hands. When Musa grew up, he was one day with Pharaoh, and he sneezed and said: "All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the worlds (الحمد لله ربّ العالمين)." Pharaoh denounced this and slapped him, saying: "What is this that you are saying?" So Musa (peace be upon him) leaped upon his beard—and Pharaoh had a long beard—and he pulled it out (فهبلها اي قلعها), causing him severe pain. Pharaoh intended to kill him, but his wife said to him: "This is a young boy (غلام حدث); he does not know what he is saying." Pharaoh said: "Yes, he knows." She said to him: "Place dates (تمراً) and embers (جمراً) before him. If he distinguishes between the date and the ember, then he is as you say." So he placed dates and embers before him and told him to eat. He reached out his hand toward the date, but Jibril (جبرئيل) came and diverted it toward the ember. He took the ember into his mouth, and his tongue was burned, and he cried out and wept. Asiyah said to Pharaoh: "Did I not tell you that he does not understand (لم يعقل)?" So he pardoned him.

(٢٩) وَاجْعَلْ لِي وَزِيراً مِنْ أَهْلِي. (29) and appoint for me a minister from my family: (٣٠) هارُونَ أَخِي ** (30) Aaron, my brother. He assists me in what You have entrusted me with. (٣١) اشْدُدْ بِهِ أَزْرِي ** (31) Strengthen my back through him, My strength (قوّتي). (٣٢) وَأَشْرِكْهُ فِي أَمْرِي ** (32) and make him a partner in my affair, And it was recited with the form of the indicative (لفظ الخبر), on the basis that both of them are the answer to the command (جواب الامر). (٣٣) كَيْ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيراً (33) so that we may glorify You greatly, (٣٤) وَنَذْكُرَكَ كَثِيراً ** (34) and remember You greatly. For cooperation (التّعاون) excites desires (الرّغبات) and leads to the multiplication and increase of goodness (الخير). (٣٥) إِنَّكَ كُنْتَ بِنا بَصِيراً ** (35) Indeed, You have been watchful of us." Knowing our circumstances (عالماً بأحوالنا), and that cooperation (التّعاون) is among the things that set us right (يصلحنا), and that Aaron is an excellent helper for me in what You have commanded me to do. (٣٦) قالَ قَدْ أُوتِيتَ سُؤْلَكَ يا مُوسى ** (36) He said, "O Moses, your request has been granted. Meaning: your requested thing (مَسئولك). (٣٧) وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلَيْكَ مَرَّةً أُخْرى ** (37) Certainly We conferred a favour on you another time, We bestowed favor upon you at another time. (٣٨) إِذْ أَوْحَيْنا إِلى أُمِّكَ ما يُوحى ** (38) when We revealed to your mother what was to be revealed: That which is not known except through revelation (الوحي). (٣٩) أَنِ اقْذِفِيهِ فِي التَّابُوتِ فَاقْذِفِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ ** (39) 'Put him in the ark, and cast it into the river, And al-qadhf (القذف) is used for casting (الإلقاء) and placing (الوضع). فَلْيُلْقِهِ الْيَمُّ بِالسَّاحِلِ يَأْخُذْهُ عَدُوٌّ لِي وَعَدُوٌّ لَهُ (and the river shall cast him on the bank, where an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his will take him). The repetition of "enemy" (عدوّ) is for emphasis (للمبالغة), or because the first is based on the reality (الواقع) and the second is based on what is expected (المتوقّع). وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِنِّي ** and I cast affection on you from Myself, Meaning: an affection originating from Me, which I planted in the hearts, such that whoever sees you can scarcely bear to be away from you. وَلِتُصْنَعَ عَلى عَيْنِي ** (and so that you might be reared under My eye). Meaning: and so that you may be raised and treated kindly, while I am your Guardian (راعيك) and your Desirer (راغبك).

(٤٠) إِذْ تَمْشِي أُخْتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى مَنْ يَكْفُلُهُ فَرَجَعْناكَ إِلى أُمِّكَ كَيْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُها ** (40) when your sister walked up and said, 'Shall I show you someone who will take care of him?' Thus We restored you to your mother so that her eye might be comforted — by meeting you — وَلا تَحْزَنَ ** (and she might not grieve.) — she, due to your separation, or you, due to her separation and the loss of her compassion (إشفاقها).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When the mother of Moses became pregnant with him, her pregnancy did not become apparent except at the time of his delivery. Pharaoh had assigned Coptic women to the women of the Children of Israel to guard them. This was because news had reached him from the Children of Israel that they were saying a man named Moses son of Imran would be born among them, and the destruction of Pharaoh and his companions would be at his hands. Pharaoh then said: "I shall surely kill their male children so that what they desire does not come to pass." He separated the men from the women and imprisoned the men in jails (المحابس). When the mother of Moses gave birth to Moses, she looked at him, grieved, became distressed, and wept, saying: "He will be slaughtered this very hour!" But Allah inclined the heart of the woman assigned to her toward him. She said to the mother of Moses: "What is wrong with you? Your color has turned yellow." She replied: "I fear that my child will be slaughtered." She said: "Do not fear." And Moses was such that no one saw him except that they loved him. This is His saying: وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِنِّي ** (and I cast upon you a love from Me). So the Coptic woman assigned to him loved him. And Allah sent down the Ark (التابوت) to the mother of Moses, and she was called upon: "Place him in the Ark, then cast him into the al-Yamm," which is the sea (البحر). وَلا تَخافِي وَلا تَحْزَنِي إِنَّا رَادُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجاعِلُوهُ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (and do not fear or grieve. We will indeed restore him to you, and We will make him one of the apostles.) So she placed him in the Ark, sealed it, and cast it into the Nile. Pharaoh had palaces on the bank of the Nile that were pleasure resorts (متنزّهات). He looked from his palace, accompanied by his wife Asiyah, and saw a dark object in the Nile, lifted by the waves and struck by the winds, until it reached the gate of Pharaoh's palace. Pharaoh ordered it to be taken. So the Ark was taken and brought up to him. When he opened it, he found a boy inside. He said: "This is an Israelite!" But Allah cast intense love for Moses into Pharaoh's heart, and likewise into the heart of Asiyah. Pharaoh intended to kill him.

Asiyah said: لا تَقْتُلُوهُ عَسى أَنْ يَنْفَعَنا أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُ وَلَداً وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** (Do not kill him. Maybe he will benefit us, or we will adopt him as a son.' And they were unaware.) — that he was Moses (peace be upon him). Pharaoh had no son. He said: "Bring him a wet nurse (ظئراً) for his upbringing." So they brought several women whose children had been killed, but he did not drink the milk of any of the women. This is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَحَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِ الْمَراضِعَ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (and We had already forbidden him to suckle at any wet nurse.) News reached his mother that Pharaoh had taken him, so she grieved and wept, just as Allah said: وَأَصْبَحَ فُؤادُ أُمِّ مُوسى فارِغاً إِنْ كادَتْ لَتُبْدِي بِهِ ** (The heart of Moses’ mother became vacant. She was about to disclose his matter,) He said: She was about to reveal his news or die. Then she controlled herself, so she was as Allah said: لَوْ لا أَنْ رَبَطْنا عَلى قَلْبِها لِتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (had We not fortified her heart so that she might be among the faithful.) Then قالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِ قُصِّيهِ ** (She said to his sister, 'Follow him.') meaning: follow him. So his sister came to him, and فَبَصُرَتْ بِهِ عَنْ جُنُبٍ (So she watched him from afar), meaning: from a distance, وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** (while they were unaware.) When Moses refused to take the breast of any of the women, Pharaoh became severely distressed. فَقالَتْ ** (So she said) — his sister — هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ يَكْفُلُونَهُ لَكُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ ناصِحُونَ** (Shall I show you someone who will take care of him for you, and they will be sincere to him?) They said: "Yes." So she brought his mother. When she took him into her lap and offered him her breast, he took it and drank. Pharaoh and his family rejoiced and honored his mother. He said to her: "Raise him for us, and we shall do such and such for you." The narrator asked him: "How long did Moses (peace be upon him) remain absent from his mother until Allah restored him to her?" He said: "Three days."

وَقَتَلْتَ نَفْساً ** (and you killed a soul,) — the soul of the Copt against whom the Israelite sought his help, as his story will come in Surat al-Qasas, if Allah, the Almighty, wills. فَنَجَّيْناكَ مِنَ الْغَمِ ** (whereupon We delivered you from distress,) — the distress (غم) of killing him, fearing the punishment of Allah and the retribution of Pharaoh, by granting forgiveness and commanding emigration to Midian. وَفَتَنَّاكَ فُتُوناً ** (and tried you with various trials.) — and We tested you with a trial (ابتلاء) or with types of trials (أنواعاً من الابتلاء), trial after trial. That is because he was born in a year when the male children were being killed, and his mother cast him into the sea, and Pharaoh intended to kill him, and during his journey, he endured what he endured, such as emigration from his homeland, separation from loved ones (الالّاف), walking on foot (راجلاً) cautiously, losing provisions (فقد الزاد), and hiring himself out for ten years, besides other things. فَلَبِثْتَ سِنِينَ فِي أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ ** (Then you remained for some years among the people of Midian.) — You remained among them for ten years, and Midian is eight stages (مراحل) away from Egypt. ثُمَّ جِئْتَ عَلى قَدَرٍ ** (Then you came [here]... according to a measure.) It is said: meaning, according to a measure (مقدار) of time in which revelation is sent to the prophets, which is the beginning of forty years. And it is said: Its meaning is that it was predetermined in My decree (قدري) and judgment (قضائي) that I would speak to you at a specific time, so you came according to that measure (القدر). يا مُوسى ** (O Moses,) It is said: He repeated it following what is the culmination of the narrative, to draw attention to that.

(٤١) وَاصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِي ** (41) and I have chosen you for Myself. And I have taken you as My protégé (صنيعتي) and My chosen one (خالصتي), and I have selected you for My love, My message, and My speech. (٤٢) اذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِآياتِي ** (42) Go, you and your brother, with My signs, with My miracles (بمعجزاتي). وَلا تَنِيا ** (and do not flag) meaning: do not weaken, nor fall short, فِي ذِكْرِي ** (in My remembrance). Do not forget Me wherever you may turn. And it was said: [It means] in conveying My remembrance and calling [people] to Me. (٤٣) اذْهَبا إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُ طَغى (43) Go to Pharaoh; he has indeed rebelled. (٤٤) فَقُولا لَهُ قَوْلاً لَيِّناً ** (44) Speak to him in a soft manner; such as هَلْ لَكَ إِلى أَنْ تَزَكَّى وَأَهْدِيَكَ إِلى رَبِّكَ فَتَخْشى ** (Is there [a way] for you to purify yourself, (18) and that I should guide you to your Lord, so that you may be wary [of Him]?) For indeed, it is an invitation in the form of an offer and counsel, lest foolishness incite him to attack you both. لَعَلَّهُ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوْ يَخْشى (perhaps he may take admonition or fear [Allah].)

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: As for His saying: فَقُولا لَهُ قَوْلاً لَيِّناً ** (Speak to him in a soft manner), meaning: address him by his kunya (كنِّيناه) and say to him, "O Abu Mus'ab," for Pharaoh was Abu Mus'ab al-Walid ibn Mus'ab. And as for His saying: لَعَلَّهُ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوْ يَخْشى ** (perhaps he may take admonition or fear [Allah]), He only said that so that Moses would be more eager (احرص) to go. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, already knew that Pharaoh would neither take admonition nor fear except upon seeing the affliction (البأس). Do you not hear the saying of Allah when He says: حَتَّى إِذا أَدْرَكَهُ الْغَرَقُ قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (until, when the drowning overtook him, he said, 'I believe that there is no god except Him in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.')? So Allah did not accept his faith and said: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ. ('Now?! While you had rebelled before and were one of the agents of corruption!')

And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition of His: Know that Allah, majestic is His praise, said to Moses when He sent him to Pharaoh: فَقُولا لَهُ قَوْلاً لَيِّناً لَعَلَّهُ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوْ يَخْشى ** (Speak to him in a soft manner; perhaps he may take admonition or fear [Allah]), but that was so that Moses would be more eager (احرص) to go.

(٤٥) قالا رَبَّنا إِنَّنا نَخافُ أَنْ يَفْرُطَ عَلَيْنا ** (They said, 'Our Lord! We are afraid that he will hasten against us) That he hastens against us with punishment, and does not proceed to the completion of the call (الدّعوة) and the manifestation of the miracle (المعجزة). [It is derived] from فرط (to hasten) when he precedes/advances. أَوْ أَنْ يَطْغى ** (or that he will transgress.') That he increases in transgression (طغياناً), so he oversteps the bounds to the extent of saying about You what is not proper, due to his audacity, his harshness, and his lack of good manners.

(٤٦) قالَ لا تَخافا إِنَّنِي مَعَكُما أَسْمَعُ وَأَرى ** (46) He said, 'Do not be afraid. I am with you, hearing and seeing. with protection (بالحفظ) and support (والنصرة). [I] hear and see what transpires between the two of you and him (Pharaoh) of speech or action (من قول أو فعل), so I will bring about in every situation that which averts his evil (شرّه) from the two of you and necessitates My support (نصرتي) for the two of you. (٤٧) فَأْتِياهُ فَقُولا إِنَّا رَسُولا رَبِّكَ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ وَلا تُعَذِّبْهُمْ قَدْ جِئْناكَ بِآيَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَالسَّلامُ عَلى مَنِ اتَّبَعَ الْهُدى ** (47) So approach him and say, 'We are the apostles of your Lord. So let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not torment them. We have certainly brought you a sign from your Lord, and peace be upon him who follows the guidance. Release them (أطلقهم) and do not torment them (وَلا تُعَذِّبْهُمْ) with difficult obligations (بالتكاليف الصعبة). We have certainly brought you a sign from your Lord with a miracle (بمعجزة) and a proof (وبرهان). And safety (والسلامة) from the punishment of Allah (من عذاب الله) is upon the guided ones (على المهتدين).

(48) إِنَّا قَدْ أُوحِيَ إِلَيْنا أَنَّ الْعَذابَ عَلى مَنْ كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّى ** (48) We have certainly been revealed to that the punishment is on him who denies and turns away.' Indeed, the punishment is upon those who deny the Messengers (الرّسل). (49) قالَ فَمَنْ رَبُّكُما يا مُوسى ** (49) He said, 'Who is your Lord, O Moses?' Meaning, after the two of them came to him and said to him what they were commanded with. He only addressed the two of them but singled out Moses for the call (النّداء) because he was the principal (الأصل), and Aaron (هرون) was his minister (وزيره) and follower (تابعه). Or, his malice (خبثه) led him to request the speech of Moses rather than the speech of his brother, due to what he knew of Aaron's eloquence (فصاحة هارون).

(٥٠) قالَ رَبُّنَا الَّذِي أَعْطى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلْقَهُ ** (50) He said, 'Our Lord is He who gave everything its creation, then guided it.' Its form and its shape which corresponds to the benefit entrusted to it. ثُمَّ هَدى (then guided it) means He made it know how it benefits from what He gave. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "There is nothing among the creation of Allah except that it knows, from its own kind (شكله), the male from the female." He was asked about the meaning of ثُمَّ هَدى ** (then guided it). He said: "He guided it to marriage (النّكاح) and fornication (السّفاح) from its own kind (شكله)." It is said that this is an answer of utmost eloquence due to its conciseness and its expression concerning all existing things (الموجودات بأسرها) according to their ranks (مراتبها), and its indication that the Self-Sufficient (الغنيّ), the Inherently Powerful (القادر بالذات), the Absolute Bestower of Bounty (المنعم على الإطلاق) is Allah, the Exalted, and that everything besides Him is dependent upon Him and relies upon Him in its essence (ذاته), attributes (صفاته), and actions (أفعاله). For this reason, the one who disbelieved was confounded, and he saw no option but to turn the speech away from him (عليه السلام).

(٥١) قالَ فَما بالُ الْقُرُونِ الْأُولى ** (51) He said, 'What about the former generations?' So what is their condition after their death, regarding happiness (السّعادة) and wretchedness (الشقاوة). (٥٢) قالَ عِلْمُها عِنْدَ رَبِّي ** (52) He said, 'Its knowledge is with my Lord.' It means that it is an unseen matter (غيب) which none knows except Allah, and I am merely a servant like you; I do not know of it except what He has informed me of. فِي كِتابٍ ** in a Book. Affirmed/recorded in the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ). لا يَضِلُّ رَبِّي وَلا يَنْسى ** My Lord neither errs nor forgets.' Error (الضَلال) is that a thing misses its place, so He is not guided to it. And forgetting (النّسيان) is that it departs such that it does not cross the mind.

(٥٣) الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ مَهْداً وَسَلَكَ لَكُمْ فِيها سُبُلاً ** (53) He who made the earth a cradle for you, and threaded through it ways for you, And He provided for you therein ways (سُبُلاً) between the mountains, the valleys, and the deserts, which you traverse from land to land so that you may reach its benefits. وَأَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَخْرَجْنا بِهِ ** and sent down water from the sky, and with it We brought forth (This is) the shift (in pronoun usage) from the third person (الغيبة) to the first person plural (التكلّم), and this has many parallels in the Quran. أَزْواجاً ** kinds (أصنافاً) مِنْ نَباتٍ شَتَّى** various kinds of vegetation.

(٥٤) كُلُوا وَارْعَوْا أَنْعامَكُمْ ** (54) Eat and pasture your cattle. على إرادة القول إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِأُولِي النُّهى ** (There are indeed signs in that for those who possess intellect.) (This is understood) on the intention of the saying. For those who possess intellects (العقول) that restrain (النّاهية) from following falsehood (الباطل) and committing abominations (القبائح). It is the plural of نهية.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "By Allah, we are ulu al-nuha (أُولُوا النُّهَى)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said that the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: "Indeed, the best among you are ulu al-nuha (أُولُوا النُّهَى)." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah, who are ulu al-nuha (أُولُوا النُّهَى)?" He said: "They are those who possess good morals (الأخلاق الحسنة) and sound forbearance (الأحلام الرزينة), maintain kinship ties (صلة الأرحام), are dutiful (البررة) to mothers and fathers, look after (المتعاهدون) the poor, the neighbors, and the orphans, feed the hungry (ويطعمون الطّعام), spread peace (السلام) throughout the world (في العالم), and pray while people are asleep (نيام) and heedless (غافلون)."

(٥٥) مِنْها خَلَقْناكُمْ ** (Out of it We created you) For the dust (التراب) is the origin of the creation of your first fathers and the first material of your bodies. وَفِيها نُعِيدُكُمْ (and into it We shall return you) through death and the decomposition (تفكيك) of the parts. وَمِنْها نُخْرِجُكُمْ تارَةً أُخْرى (and out of it We shall bring you forth another time) by composing your fragmented parts (اجزائكم المفتتة) that are mixed with the dust (بالتّراب) into the previous forms (الصّور السابقة) and returning the souls (الأرواح) to them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the drop (النطفة) falls into the womb (الرّحم), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sends an angel, so he takes from the soil (التربة) in which [the person] will be buried, and mixes (فماثها) it into the drop (النّطفة). Thus, his heart continues to yearn (يحنّ) for it until he is buried in it.

(٥٦) وَلَقَدْ أَرَيْناهُ آياتِنا كُلَّها فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبى ** (And We certainly showed him all Our signs, but he denied and refused.) وَلَقَدْ أَرَيْناهُ آياتِنا ** (And We certainly showed him Our signs) — We made him see them (بصّرناه ايّاها) and informed him of their validity (وعرّفناه صحّتها). كُلَّها فَكَذَّبَ ** (all of them, but he denied) — due to the excessiveness of his obstinacy (من فرط عناده). وَأَبى ** (and refused) — faith (الايمان) and obedience (والطاعة) because of his arrogance (لعتوّه). (٥٧) قالَ أَجِئْتَنا لِتُخْرِجَنا مِنْ أَرْضِنا بِسِحْرِكَ يا مُوسى ** (He said, 'Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?) قالَ أَجِئْتَنا لِتُخْرِجَنا مِنْ أَرْضِنا ** (He said, 'Have you come to us to drive us out of our land) — the land of Egypt (أرض مصر). بِسِحْرِكَ يا مُوسى ** (with your magic, O Moses?) — This was an excuse (تعلّل) from him, and it appears from his speech that he feared him (Moses) might overcome him regarding his kingdom (ان يغلبه على ملكه). (٥٨) فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحْرٍ مِثْلِهِ فَاجْعَلْ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكَ مَوْعِداً لا نُخْلِفُهُ نَحْنُ وَلا أَنْتَ مَكاناً سُوىً ** (We will certainly bring you a magic like it. So appoint a tryst between us and you, which neither we nor you will fail to keep, in a central place.') فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحْرٍ مِثْلِهِ ** (We will certainly bring you a magic like it) — like your magic (مثل سحرك). فَاجْعَلْ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكَ مَوْعِداً ** (So appoint a tryst between us and you) — meaning a promise (وعداً). لا نُخْلِفُهُ نَحْنُ وَلا أَنْتَ مَكاناً سُوىً ** (which neither we nor you will fail to keep, in a central place.') — It was said: meaning a midpoint (منتصفاً) whose distance to us and to you is equal (يستوي مسافته إلينا وإليك). And it was recited with the dammah on the sīn (بضمّ السّين).

(٥٩) The Almighty's saying: قالَ مَوْعِدُكُمْ يَوْمُ الزِّينَةِ ** (He said, 'Your tryst shall be the Day of the Festival) And it is a day of festival (عيد) which they had every year. And He only specified it so that the truth (الحقّ) might become manifest and falsehood (الباطل) might perish before the eyes of the witnesses (الأشهاد), and so that this might spread throughout the regions (الاقطار). And the Almighty's saying: وَأَنْ يُحْشَرَ النَّاسُ ضُحًى ** (and that the people be mustered in the forenoon.') And the gathering of the people in the forenoon (ضحىً).

(٦٠) فَتَوَلَّى فِرْعَوْنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيْدَهُ ثُمَّ أَتى ** (60) Thereat Pharaoh turned away, and gathered his stratagem, then he came. [The meaning of] فَجَمَعَ كَيْدَهُ ** (and gathered his stratagem) is what he used for stratagem, namely the sorcerers (السّحرة) and their tools (وآلاتهم). ثُمَّ أَتى ** (then he came) [refers to] the appointed time (الموعد). (٦١) قالَ لَهُمْ مُوسى وَيْلَكُمْ لا تَفْتَرُوا عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً فَيُسْحِتَكُمْ بِعَذابٍ وَقَدْ خابَ مَنِ افْتَرى (61) Moses said to them, 'Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, or He will exterminate you with a punishment, and disappointed is he who fabricates a lie.' [The meaning of] لا تَفْتَرُوا عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (Do not fabricate a lie against Allah) is by claiming His signs (آياته) are sorcery (سحراً), فَيُسْحِتَكُمْ بِعَذابٍ (or He will exterminate you with a punishment), meaning He will destroy you (فيهلككم) and uproot you (ويستأصلكم) with it. And it was read with the dammah on the yā’ (وقرئ بضم الياء). وَقَدْ خابَ مَنِ افْتَرى (and disappointed is he who fabricates a lie.) (٦٢) فَتَنازَعُوا أَمْرَهُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ وَأَسَرُّوا النَّجْوى ** (62) So they disputed their matter among themselves, and kept their counsel secret. It is said that [the meaning is] that the sorcerers (السّحرة) disputed concerning the matter of Moses when they heard his speech, and some of them said: "This is not the speech of sorcerers (السّحرة)." وَأَسَرُّوا النَّجْوى ** (and kept their counsel secret) means the sorcerers (السحرة). It is said that their secret counsel (نجواهم) was: "If Moses defeats us, we will follow him; and if he is a sorcerer, we will defeat him; and if he is from Heaven (السّماء), then the command belongs to Him (فله امر)."

(٦٣) قالُوا إِنْ هذانِ لَساحِرانِ ** (63) They said, 'These two are indeed magicians, Pharaoh and his people said it. And this (reading) is according to the dialect (لغة) of Banu Harith ibn Ka'b, for they made the alif (الالف) for the dual (التثنية) and vocalized the dual (المثنّى) by estimation (تقديراً). And it was read (وقرئ) as إنْ هذان on the basis that إنْ is the lightened (المخففة) form (of إنّ), and the lām (اللّام) is the distinguishing (الفارقة) or the negating (النافية) one, or the lām (اللّام) is in the meaning of إلّا (except). And it was read (وقرئ) as هذين, and this is clear (ظاهر). يُرِيدانِ أَنْ يُخْرِجاكُمْ مِنْ أَرْضِكُمْ ** by seizing control (الاستيلاء) of it بِسِحْرِهِما وَيَذْهَبا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ الْمُثْلى ** (And to do away with your most excellent way), meaning: with your doctrine (مذهبكم) which is the most excellent (أفضل) of doctrines (المذاهب), or (it means) with the people of your way (أهل طريقتكم), and the notables (وجوه) of your people, and your dignitaries (اشرافكم).

(٦٤) فَأَجْمِعُوا كَيْدَكُمْ ** (64) So determine upon your stratagem, So resolve upon it (فَازْمَعُوهُ) and make it a matter of consensus (مُجْمَعًا عَلَيْهِ) from which not one of you deviates. And it was recited (فَاجْمَعُوا) [meaning: So gather]. And this is supported by His saying: فَجَمَعَ كَيْدَهُ ثُمَّ ائْتُوا صَفًّا ** (60) So he gathered his stratagem, then they came in a row. Lined up in ranks (مُصْطَفِّينَ), because it is more awe-inspiring in the hearts of the beholders. It was said that they were seventy thousand, with every one of them a rope and a staff. And approach him with a single approach. **وَقَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْيَوْمَ مَنِ اسْتَعْلى ** and successful today is he who prevails." He who overcomes attains the desired goal.

(٦٥) قالُوا يا مُوسى إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَلْقى ** (65) They said, 'O Moses, either you cast, or we shall be the first to cast.' Meaning, after they came, observing proper etiquette (الأدب). (٦٦) قالَ بَلْ أَلْقُوا ** (66) He said, 'Rather, you cast.' [This was] meeting etiquette with etiquette (مقابلة ادب بأدب), and showing no concern (عدم مبالاة) for their magic, and so that they might exert their utmost effort (بأقصى وسعهم), then Allah would manifest His authority (سلطانه). نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْباطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ فَإِذا حِبالُهُمْ وَعِصِيُّهُمْ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ سِحْرِهِمْ أَنَّها تَسْعى ** (We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it smashes its head, and behold, falsehood vanishes.) And behold, their ropes and their staffs appeared to him, by their magic, as if they were moving swiftly. Meaning, so they cast [their objects]. And it is said that they had smeared them with mercury (الزّيبق), so when the sun struck them, they writhed (اضطربت), and it was imagined (فخيّل) that they were moving. And the word تخيّل was read with the tā' (التّاء) based on the active voice construction (على بناء الفاعل).

(٦٧) فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ خِيفَةً مُوسى ** (67) Moses felt a fear within himself. So he concealed a fear within it (his soul). In Nahj al-Balaghah: Moses did not feel fear for himself; rather, he feared the dominance of the ignorant and the states (دُوَل) of misguidance. (٦٨) قُلْنا لا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْأَعْلى ** (68) We said, 'Do not fear. It is you who will be superior.' This is a justification (تَعْلِيل) for the prohibition (of fear) and an emphatic confirmation (تَقْرِير) of his victory. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: When Moses (peace be upon him) cast down his staff, فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ خِيفَةً ** (Moses felt a fear within himself), he said: "O Allah, I ask You by the right of Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد) that You grant me security." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: لا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْأَعْلى.** (Do not fear. It is you who will be superior.)

(٦٩) وَأَلْقِ ما فِي يَمِينِكَ تَلْقَفْ ما صَنَعُوا ** (69) And cast what is in your right hand; it will swallow what they have crafted. It will swallow it by the power of Allah the Almighty. And it was recited with rafʿ (بالرّفع) and with takhfif (بالتّخفيف). ما صَنَعُوا ** That which they forged (زوّروا) and fabricated (وافتعلوا). كَيْدُ سَاحِرٍ وَلا يُفْلِحُ السَّاحِرُ حَيْثُ أَتى ** [It is] but a sorcerer’s trick, and the sorcerer will not be successful wherever he may come. Wherever he was and wherever he approached (أقبل).

(٧٠) فَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ سُجَّداً ** (70) Then the magicians fell down prostrating i.e., so He cast, and it swallowed, and it became certain for the magicians that it is not magic, but rather it is from the signs (آيات) and miracles (معجزات) of Allah. So that threw them onto their faces, prostrating to Allah, as repentance for what they had done and glorification of what they saw. قالُوا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّ هارُونَ وَمُوسى. They said, 'We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.'**

(٧١) قالَ آمَنْتُمْ لَهُ ** (71) He said, 'Did you believe in him meaning, in Musa. The lām (لَهُ) is used because the verb (آمَنْتُمْ) implies the meaning of following (الاتّباع). It was also recited without the hamzah (آمَنْتُمْ).

**قَبْلَ أَنْ آذَنَ لَكُمْ ** before I permitted you? regarding believing in him.

إِنَّهُ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ** He is indeed your chief, meaning, your great one (عظيمكم) in your craft, the most knowledgeable among you concerning it, and your master (أستاذكم) WHO TAUGHT YOU MAGIC. And you conspired (تواطأتم) regarding what you have done.

**فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ مِنْ خِلافٍ ** I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, (meaning) the right hand and the left foot.

وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ فِي جُذُوعِ النَّخْلِ وَلَتَعْلَمُنَّ أَيُّنا ** and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of the palm trees, and you will surely know which of us He means by this himself and Musa, or the Lord (رَبّ) of Musa.

أَشَدُّ عَذاباً وَأَبْقى ** is more severe in punishment and more lasting.' (meaning) more enduring in retribution (عقاباً).

(٧٢) قالُوا لَنْ نُؤْثِرَكَ ** (72) They said, 'We will never prefer you لن نختارك عَلى ما جاءَنا ** We will not choose you عَلى ما جاءَنا ** (over what has come to us) with it (the miracles) by Moses, or (it refers to) the pronoun hidden in (جاء) because of what مِنَ الْبَيِّناتِ ** of manifest proofs, the clear miracles. وَالَّذِي فَطَرَنا ** and by Him who originated us! This is grammatically connected (عطف) to (ما جاءَنا) (what has come to us), or it is an oath (قسم). فَاقْضِ ما أَنْتَ قاضٍ ** So decree whatever you will decree. (It means) what you are decreeing (قاضيه), meaning: what you are doing (صانعه) or judging (حاكمه). إِنَّما تَقْضِي هذِهِ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا You can only decree in this worldly life. You only do what you desire (تهواه) or judge according to what you see in this world (الدّنيا), but the Hereafter is better and more lasting. Thus, it is like a justification (كالتعليل) for what preceded it and a preparation (التمهيد) for what follows it.

(٧٣) إِنَّا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّنا لِيَغْفِرَ لَنا خَطايانا ** (73) We have indeed believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our errors (This refers to) disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). وَما أَكْرَهْتَنا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ السِّحْرِ ** and what you compelled us to do of sorcery. (This refers to the sorcery used) in opposing the miracle (المعجزة). In al-Jawami': It is narrated that they said to Pharaoh: "Show us Moses while he is sleeping." So they found him, and the staff (العصا) was guarding him. They said: "This is not sorcery (سحر), for when a sorcerer sleeps, his sorcery is nullified." But he refused except that they oppose him (Moses). وَاللهُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقى ** And Allah is better and more lasting. (He is) better in recompense (جزاء), or better in reward (ثواباً) and more lasting in punishment (عقاباً).

(74) إِنَّهُ ** The matter is مَنْ يَأْتِ رَبَّهُ مُجْرِماً ** by dying upon his disbelief (كفره) and his disobedience (عصيانه), then surely for him is Hell, where he will not die so that he may rest وَلا يَحْيى ** a pleasant life (حياة مهنّأة). إِنَّهُ مَنْ يَأْتِ رَبَّهُ مُجْرِماً فَإِنَّ لَهُ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَا (٧٤)** (74) Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal—there is hell for him, where he will neither die nor live.

(75) وَمَنْ يَأْتِهِ مُؤْمِناً قَدْ عَمِلَ الصَّالِحاتِ ** in the world (الدنيا), فَأُولئِكَ لَهُمُ الدَّرَجاتُ الْعُلى ** the elevated stations (المنازل الرّفيعة). وَمَنْ يَأْتِهِ مُؤْمِناً قَدْ عَمِلَ الصَّالِحَاتِ فَأُولَئِكَ لَهُمُ الدَّرَجَاتُ الْعُلَى (٧٥) (75) But whoever comes to Him as a faithful one, having done righteous deeds—for such there will be the highest ranks.

(76) جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَذلِكَ جَزاءُ مَنْ تَزَكَّى ** whoever purified himself from the filth (ادناس) of disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). And the three verses possibly belong to the speech of the sorcerers (السّحرة), or they possibly belong to the beginning of speech from Allah. جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا وَذَلِكَ جَزَاءُ مَنْ تَزَكَّى (٧٦)** (76) Gardens of Eden, with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. That is the reward of those who purify themselves.

(٧٧) وَلَقَدْ أَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبادِي ** (77) Certainly We revealed to Moses: 'Set out with My servants by night, meaning: from Egypt. فَاضْرِبْ لَهُمْ ** (then strike for them) meaning: make for them طَرِيقاً فِي الْبَحْرِ يَبَساً ** (a dry path in the sea) meaning: (يابساً). لا تَخافُ دَرَكاً ** (you will not fear being overtaken) meaning: safe from the enemy overtaking you. And it was read (لا تخف) (do not fear). وَلا تَخْشى (nor be afraid) is an independent clause (استيناف) or a conjunction (عطف).

(٧٨) فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ بِجُنُودِهِ ** (78) Pharaoh pursued them with his hosts. Pharaoh pursued them himself, and his hosts were with him. فَغَشِيَهُمْ مِنَ الْيَمِّ ما غَشِيَهُمْ ** Then the sea overwhelmed them with what overwhelmed them. [This refers to] that whose story has not been heard, nor is its essence (كنهه) known except by Allah. In this is hyperbole (مبالغة) and conciseness (وجازة). (٧٩) وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُ وَما هَدى (79) Pharaoh led his people astray, and did not guide [them]. Ibn Tawus (may Allah have mercy on him) narrated from Tafsir al-Kalbi, from Ibn Abbas, that Jibril said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a narration concerning the state of Pharaoh and his people: He only said to his people: أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى ** (24) I am your Lord, the most exalted. when he reached the sea and saw that the path within it had dried up. So he said to his people: "Do you see the sea? It has dried up out of fear of me (من فرقي)." So they believed him when they saw that. That is the Almighty's saying: وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُ وَما هَدى (Pharaoh led his people astray, and did not guide [them]). The completion of the story will come in Surat al-Shu'ara.

(٨٠) يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (80) O Children of Israel! This is an address to them after their rescue from the sea and the destruction of Pharaoh, based on the implied (word) 'We said' (قُلْنَا), or (it is addressed) to those among them during the time of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning what was done to their forefathers. قَدْ أَنْجَيْناكُمْ مِنْ عَدُوِّكُمْ (Pharaoh and his people) We certainly delivered you from your enemy وَواعَدْناكُمْ جانِبَ الطُّورِ الْأَيْمَنَ ** and We made a tryst with you on the right side of the Mount for the intimate conversation (مُنَاجَاة) of Moses (عليه السلام) and the sending down of the Torah upon him. And it was read (قُرِئَ) as أَنْجَيْتُكُمْ and وَاعَدْتُكُمْ. وَنَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَنَّ وَالسَّلْوى ** and We sent down to you the manna and the quails meaning, in the wilderness (التِّيه), just as its story preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٨١) كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّباتِ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ ** (81) (Eat of the good things We have provided you,) its delicious things, وَلا تَطْغَوْا فِيهِ ** (and do not transgress in it,) by failing to thank Him, and by exceeding what Allah has limited for you therein, such as extravagance (السرف), insolence (البطر), and withholding from the deserving (المستحق), فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبِي ** (or My wrath shall descend upon you.) so My punishment (عذابي) becomes binding upon you and becomes due for you, وَمَنْ يَحْلِلْ عَلَيْهِ غَضَبِي فَقَدْ ** (And he on whom My wrath descends has certainly) fallen and perished. And the words يحلّ and يحلل were read with a ḍammah (بالضمّ).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, "What is that wrath (الغضب)?" He said: "It is the punishment (العقاب)." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, whoever claims that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, moved (زال) from one thing to another has described Him with the attribute (صفة) of a creature. Indeed, nothing provokes Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, nor does anything change Him."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): what is close to it.

(82) وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ثُمَّ اهْتَدى (82) Indeed, I am most forgiving toward him who repents and believes and acts righteously, then follows the guidance.

وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تابَ from polytheism (الشرك) وَآمَنَ in what belief (الإِيمان) in it is obligatory وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ثُمَّ اهْتَدى to the wilāyah (ولاية) of the People of the House (peace be upon them).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "Do you not see how He stipulated it? Repentance (التوبة), faith (الإيمان), and righteous action (العمل الصالح) did not benefit him until he followed the guidance (اهتدى). By Allah, even if he strove to act, it would not be accepted from him until he follows the guidance (يهتدي)." He was asked: "To whom? May Allah make me your ransom." He replied: "To us."

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny), that He said to Ali (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "Indeed, whoever went astray, went astray from you. And no one will be guided to Allah who is not guided to you and to your wilāyah (ولايتك). This is the saying of my Lord, the Mighty and the Majestic: وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ [the rest of] the verse, meaning: to your wilāyah (ولايتك)."

In al-Majma' and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: ثُمَّ اهْتَدى to our wilāyah (ولايتنا), the People of the House. By Allah, if a man were to worship Allah throughout his life between the Rukn (الركن) and the Maqam (المقام), then died without bringing our wilāyah (ولايتنا), Allah would cast him face down into the Fire.

In al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: ثُمَّ اهْتَدى, He said: "To us, the People of the House."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ثُمَّ اهْتَدى, He said: "To our wilāyah (ولايتنا)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), while He was facing the House (البيت), He said: "Indeed, people were commanded only to come to these stones (الأحجار) and circumambulate them, and then to come to us and inform us of their wilāyah (ولايتهم) for us. This is the saying of Allah, the Exalted: وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ثُمَّ اهْتَدى (And indeed, I am most forgiving toward him who repents and believes and acts righteously, then follows the guidance)." Then He gestured with His hand toward His chest: "To our wilāyah (ولايتنا)."

And in al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "This verse has an interpretation (تفسير), and that interpretation indicates that Allah does not accept any action from anyone unless he meets Him having fulfilled that interpretation and what He stipulated therein upon the believers."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "Indeed, you will not be righteous (صالحين) until you know, and you will not know until you affirm the truth (تصدّقوا), and you will not affirm the truth until you submit to four doors (أبواباً أربعة), such that the first of them is not rectified except by the last of them. The companions of the three (أصحاب الثلاثة) went astray and wandered in great confusion. Indeed, Allah, the Exalted, accepts nothing but righteous action (العمل الصالح), and Allah accepts nothing but the fulfillment of the stipulations (الشروط) and covenants (العهود). So, whoever fulfills Allah, the Exalted's, stipulation and employs what He described in His covenant, he attains what is with Him and completes His promise. Indeed, Allah, the Exalted, informed the servants of the paths of guidance (الهدى) and established beacons (المنار) for them therein, and informed them how they should traverse them, so He said: إِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ثُمَّ اهْتَدى (Indeed, I am most forgiving toward him who repents and believes and acts righteously, then follows the guidance). And He said: إِنَّما يَتَقَبَّلُ اللهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ (Allah accepts only from the Godwary). So, whoever is Godwary (اتّقى الله) regarding what He commanded him, he meets Allah as a believer in what Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny) brought. Far, far off! A people missed the mark and died before they followed the guidance (يهتدوا), and they thought they had believed, yet they committed polytheism (أشركوا) without knowing it. Indeed, whoever approaches houses through their doors is guided, and whoever takes another way traverses the path of ruin (الرّدى). Allah connected obedience to the one entrusted with His command (ولّى أمره) with obedience to His Messenger, and obedience to His Messenger with obedience to Him. So, whoever abandons obedience to the guardians of the command (ولاة الامر) has not obeyed Allah nor His Messenger. And this is the affirmation (الإقرار) of what was sent down from Allah, the Exalted."

I say: He referred by the four doors (الأبواب الأربعة) to repentance from polytheism (الشرك), belief in the Oneness (الوحدانية), righteous action (العمل الصالح), and following the guidance (الاهتداء) to the Proofs (الحجج) (peace be upon them), as will become clear later. And the companions of the three (أصحاب الثلاثة) is a reference to those who were not guided to the Proofs (الحجج). And the stipulations (الشروط) and covenants (العهود) are a metaphor (كناية) for the four mentioned matters, as they are stipulations for forgiveness (المغفرة) and covenants (عهود). And His saying: "So, whoever is Godwary (فمن اتّقى الله)" means: from polytheism (الشرك) in His command.

(٨٣) وَما أَعْجَلَكَ عَنْ قَوْمِكَ يا مُوسى. (83) "And what has made you hasten away from your people, O Moses?" (٨٤) قالَ هُمْ أُولاءِ عَلى أَثَرِي ** (84) He said, 'They are following close on my track, I did not precede them except by a few steps (خُطى يسيرة) which are not usually counted, وَعَجِلْتُ إِلَيْكَ رَبِّ لِتَرْضى ** and I have hastened to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased.' For indeed, hastening to comply with Your command and fulfilling Your covenant necessitates Your pleasure (مرضاتك). In مصباح الشريعة, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The yearning one (المشتاق) does not desire food, nor find pleasure in drink, nor enjoy sleep, nor feel intimacy with a close friend (حميماً), nor seek shelter in a house, nor reside in a settled place (عمراناً), nor wear clothing, nor settle down in stability (قراراً). And he worships Allah night and day, hoping that he may reach that which he yearns for, and he supplicates to Him (يناجيه) with the tongue of his yearning, expressing what is in his innermost being (سريرته), just as Allah informed about Moses son of Imran regarding the appointed time with his Lord, by His saying: **وَعَجِلْتُ إِلَيْكَ رَبِّ لِتَرْضى ** (and I have hastened to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased). And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم explained concerning his (Moses') state that he neither ate, nor drank, nor slept, nor desired anything of that during his going and coming for forty days, out of yearning for his Lord.

(85) قالَ فَإِنَّا قَدْ فَتَنَّا قَوْمَكَ مِنْ بَعْدِكَ ** (85) He said, 'We have tested your people in your absence, We tested them (ابتليناهم) with the worship of the calf after your departure from among them. وَأَضَلَّهُمُ السَّامِرِيُ And the Samiri (السَّامِرِيُ) led them astray by taking the calf and calling (الدّعاء) to its worship. (86) فَرَجَعَ مُوسى إِلى قَوْمِهِ ** (86) So Moses returned to his people after he completed the forty [nights] and took the Torah (التوراة), angry at them. غَضْبانَ أَسِفاً ** angry and sorrowful. Sorrowful (حزيناً) because of what they had done. قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَلَمْ يَعِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ وَعْداً حَسَناً ** He said, 'O my people! Did not your Lord promise you a good promise? [The good promise was] that He would give you the Torah (التّوراة) in which there is guidance (هُدىً) and light (نور). أَفَطالَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْعَهْدُ ** Did the term seem too long to you, meaning the time (الزّمان), the time of his separation (مفارقته) from them? أَمْ أَرَدْتُمْ أَنْ يَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ ** or did you desire that wrath from your Lord should descend upon you, [The meaning of يَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ is] that wrath (غَضَبٌ) from your Lord should become incumbent upon you (يجب عليكم) by worshipping that which is an example of stupidity (الغباوة), غَضَبٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُمْ مَوْعِدِي that you broke your promise to me?' [The promise they broke was] your promise to me (وعدكم ايّاي) to remain firm (بالثبوت) upon faith (الايمان) in Allah and guidance (الهدى), and to uphold what I commanded you with.

(٨٧) قالُوا ما أَخْلَفْنا مَوْعِدَكَ بِمَلْكِنا ** (87) They said, 'We did not break our appointment with you by our own power, meaning that we possessed (ملكنا) our affair. That is, if we had been left to our own affair and the Samiri had not tempted us, we would not have broken (the appointment). And it is the masdar (مصدر) of ملكت الشيء (I possessed the thing), vocalized with the three vowel movements (مثلّثاً), and it was read with fathah and with ḍammah. وَلكِنَّا حُمِّلْنا أَوْزاراً مِنْ زِينَةِ الْقَوْمِ ** (but we were laden with burdens of the people's ornaments) burdens (احمالاً) of the jewelry of the Copts (القبط) which we had borrowed from them, and which the sea cast upon the shore after their drowning. And it was read ḥumalna (حملنا) with fathah and takhfif (lightening). فَقَذَفْناها ** (and we cast them) meaning, into the fire. فَكَذلِكَ أَلْقَى السَّامِرِيُ ** (Then the Samiri cast likewise) meaning, what he had with him of it (the ornaments).

(88) فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلاً جَسَداً (88) Then he produced for them a calf, a mere body, from those melted ornaments, لَهُ خُوارٌ ** (having a lowing sound), the sound of a calf. فَقالُوا ** (Then they said)—meaning the Samiri and those who were seduced by it the moment they saw it— هذا إِلهُكُمْ وَإِلهُ مُوسى فَنَسِيَ ** (This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot.’) It was said: So Moses forgot Him (or it) and went seeking Him (or it) near al-Tur (Mount Sinai). Or فَنَسِيَ ** (he forgot) refers to the Samiri, meaning he abandoned what he was upon, which was the manifestation of faith (إظهار الإِيمان).

(٨٩) أَفَلا يَرَوْنَ ** or do they not know أَلَّا يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِمْ قَوْلاً ** that it does not return speech (كلاماً) to them, nor does it send back a reply (جواباً) to them وَلا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ ضَرًّا وَلا نَفْعاً (89) Do they not see that it does not return them any remark, nor does it control any harm or benefit for them? (٩٠) وَلَقَدْ قالَ لَهُمْ هارُونُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** before the return of Moses يا قَوْمِ إِنَّما فُتِنْتُمْ بِهِ ** by the calf (بالعجل) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ الرَّحْمنُ ** and none other (لا غير) فَاتَّبِعُونِي وَأَطِيعُوا أَمْرِي ** in remaining firm upon the religion (في الثّبات على الدّين). (90) Certainly Aaron had said to them earlier, 'O my people, you are only being tested by it. Indeed your Lord is the All-beneficent, so follow me and obey my command.'

(٩١) قالُوا لَنْ نَبْرَحَ عَلَيْهِ عاكِفِينَ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنا مُوسى (91) They said, 'We will not cease to cling to it until Moses returns to us.' They said, 'We will not cease to cling to it): meaning, to the calf and its worship. clinging to it): meaning, remaining (upon it). until Moses returns to us.' Al-Qummi (narrates): So they intended (harm) against Harun, and he fled from them. And they remained in that state until the appointed time of Moses—forty nights—was completed. When it was the tenth day of Dhul-Hijjah (ذي الحجّة), Allah sent down upon him the Tablets, containing the Torah and what was needed from the rulings of conduct (احكام السير) and the stories (القصص). Then Allah revealed to Moses (peace be upon him): فَإِنَّا قَدْ فَتَنَّا قَوْمَكَ مِنْ بَعْدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ السَّامِرِيُ ** (Indeed, We have tested your people in your absence, and the Samiri has led them astray.') And they worshipped the calf, and it had a lowing sound (خُوار). So he (Moses) said: "O Lord, the calf is from the Samiri, but the lowing sound (الخوار) is from Whom?" He (Allah) said: "From Me, O Moses. When I saw that they had turned away from Me towards the calf, I desired to increase them in trial (فتنة)." Then Moses returned to his people, as Allah recounted.

(92) قالَ يا هارُونُ ** (He said, 'O Aaron!) Meaning: Moses said to him when he returned: ما مَنَعَكَ إِذْ رَأَيْتَهُمْ ضَلُّوا ** (What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,) by worshipping the calf. (93) أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ ** (from following me?') Meaning: [from following me] in anger for the sake of Allah and fighting those who disbelieved in Him, and that you should come after me and join me. The لا (lā) is extra (مزيدة), just as in His saying: ما مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ أَفَعَصَيْتَ أَمْرِي ** (What prevented you from prostrating? Did you disobey My command?)—[meaning] with firmness in religion and defending it.

al-Qummi (narrates): Then he threw down the Tablets (الالواح) and seized his brother's beard and head, dragging him towards himself, and said: ما مَنَعَكَ. (What prevented you...)

(٩٤) **قالَ يَا بْنَ أُمَ ** (94) He said, 'Son of my mother! Do not seize my beard or my head. I feared lest you should say, "You caused a division among the Children of Israel, and did not observe my word."'

He specified the mother seeking compassion (استعطافاً) and tenderness (وترقيقاً). لا تَأْخُذْ بِلِحْيَتِي وَلا بِرَأْسِي إِنِّي خَشِيتُ أَنْ تَقُولَ فَرَّقْتَ بَيْنَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** [This would happen] if I fought some of them against others. وَلَمْ تَرْقُبْ قَوْلِي ** when You said اخْلُفْنِي فِي قَوْمِي وَأَصْلِحْ ** (Take my place among my people, and set things right). For indeed, setting things right (الإصلاح) lay in preserving lives (حفظ الدّماء) and managing affairs gently (المداراة) among them until You returned to them, so that You might then remedy the matter according to Your judgment (برأيك).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): He was asked why [Musa] seized his head, pulling him towards himself, and his beard, when [Harun] had no sin regarding their taking the calf and worshipping it. He (عليه السلام) replied: "He only did that because he did not leave them when they did that, nor did he join Musa. Had he left them, the punishment would have descended upon them. Do you not see that [Musa] said to Harun: مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذْ رَأَيْتَهُمْ ضَلُّوا أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ أَفَعَصَيْتَ أَمْرِي (What prevented you, when you saw them going astray, from following me? Did you disobey My command?)? Harun said: 'Had I done that, they would have dispersed (لتفرّقوا).'"

(٩٥) **قالَ فَما خَطْبُكَ يا سامِرِيُ ** (95) He said, 'What was your business, O Samiri?' Then [Musa] turned to him and said to him, disapprovingly: "What was your aim in doing that, and what compelled you to do it?"

(٩٦) قالَ بَصُرْتُ بِما لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا بِهِ ** (96) He said, 'I perceived what they did not perceive. So I took a handful from the track of the Messenger, and cast it. Thus did my soul prompt me.' I knew what they did not know, or I realized what they did not realize. This was that the Messenger who came to you was spiritual (روحاني), and his trace would not touch anything except that He would bring it to life. And [the word] لم تبصروا was read in the address form (على الخطاب). فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةً مِنْ أَثَرِ الرَّسُولِ ** Al-Qummi said: meaning from beneath the hoof of the steed (رَمَكه) of Gabriel (جبرئيل) in the sea. فَنَبَذْتُها ** meaning I held it, then I cast it into the belly of the calf. This story has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah and then in Surat al-A'raf. وَكَذلِكَ سَوَّلَتْ لِي نَفْسِي ** That is, it beautified [it for me]. Al-Qummi said: So Musa brought out the calf, burned it with fire, and cast it into the sea.

(٩٧) قالَ فَاذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي الْحَياةِ ** (He said, 'Begone! Your lot in life shall be) (a punishment for what you have done) أَنْ تَقُولَ لا مِساسَ ** (that you will say, "No touching!") (out of fear that someone might touch you and you would be afflicted by fever (الْحُمَى). And whoever touches you, people will avoid him and he will avoid them, and you will be an outcast (طَرِيداً), solitary (وَحِيداً), like a startled wild animal (كَالْوَحْشِيِّ النَّافِر).) القمّيّ (said): It means as long as you are alive, and this sign (الْعَلَامَة) remains established among your descendants: أَنْ تَقُولَ لا مِساسَ ** (that you will say, "No touching!") so that they may know that you are Samaritans (سَامِرِيَّة) and people will not be deceived by you. They are known in Egypt (مِصْر) and Syria (الشَّام) until this hour as "No touching" (لَا مِسَاس). He said: Then Moses intended to kill the Samaritan, but Allah revealed to Him: "Do not kill him, O Moses, for he is generous (سَخِيّ)." In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That Moses intended [to kill him]—the rest of the narration (الْحَدِيث). وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِداً ** (and there is a tryst for you) (in the Hereafter) لَنْ تُخْلَفَهُ ** (which you will not fail to keep.) (Allah will not fail to keep it for you, and He will fulfill it for you in the Hereafter after He has punished you in this world. And it was recited with a kasrah on the lām (لَنْ تُخْلِفَهُ), meaning: you will not fail to keep the appointment made to him, and you will inevitably come to it.) وَانْظُرْ إِلى إِلهِكَ الَّذِي ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عاكِفاً ** (Look at your god to which you kept clinging:) (you remained persistent in its worship (ظَلِلْتَ عَلَى عِبَادَتِهِ مُقِيماً), and the first lām (ل) was omitted for lightness (تَخْفِيفاً)) لَنُحَرِّقَنَّهُ ** (We will certainly burn it) (meaning, with fire (بِالنَّار).) In al-Jawami': And it was recited لَنَحْرِقَنَّه (with a fatḥah on the ḥā'), and this is the recitation of Ali (قِرَاءَةُ عَلِيّ), and its meaning is: We will certainly file it down (لَنُبَرِّدَنَّهُ) with a file (بِالْمِبْرَد). He said: And it is permissible that لَنُحَرِّقَنَّه (with a ḍammah on the ḥā') is an exaggeration of حَرَقَ (ḥaraqa) when it means to file down (بَرَدَ). He said: And this recitation (الْقِرَاءَة) indicates that it was gold and silver and did not become a living creature (حَيَوَاناً). I say: It has preceded that he filed down (بَرَدَ) the calf, then burned it with fire, and scattered it in the sea (الْيَمّ). And in one narration: Its filings (سُحَالَتُهُ) were scattered in the water. ثُمَّ لَنَنْسِفَنَّهُ ** (then we will certainly scatter it) (We will scatter it as ashes (رَمَاداً) or as filings (مَبْرُوداً)) فِي الْيَمِّ نَسْفاً ** (in the sea, scattering it thoroughly.) (so that nothing of it remains. The purpose is to increase the punishment and expose the foolishness (غَبَاوَة) of those who were tempted by it.)

(٩٨) إِنَّما إِلهُكُمُ اللهُ (98) Your God is only Allah, The One deserving of your worship, الَّذِي لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ besides Whom there is no god, The One Whom no one resembles or approaches (يُدَانِيهِ) in the perfection of knowledge and power. وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْماً He comprehends everything in knowledge. His knowledge encompasses everything that is proper to be known, not the calf which is molded and burned, and even if it were alive in itself, it would be an example of stupidity (الغباوة).

(٩٩) كَذلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ ما قَدْ سَبَقَ ** (99) Thus do We recount to you the accounts of what has passed. (This is) from the accounts (أخبار) of past matters (الأمور الماضية) and bygone nations (الأمم الدارجة), as an enlightenment (تبصرة) for you, an increase in your knowledge, a multiplication of your miracles (معجزاتك), and an admonition (تنبيهاً) and reminder (تذكيراً) for those who seek insight (المستبصرين) among your nation (امّتك). وَقَدْ آتَيْناكَ مِنْ لَدُنَّا ذِكْراً ** And We have certainly given you a Reminder from Our presence. (It is) a Book (كتاباً) comprising these narratives (الأقاصيص) and accounts (الاخبار), worthy of reflection (التفكّر) and consideration (الاعتبار).

(١٠٠) مَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَحْمِلُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وِزْراً ** (100) Whoever turns away from it, he shall carry a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection, a heavy, grievous punishment (عقوبة ثقيلة فادحة) for his disbelief and his sins. (١٠١) خالِدِينَ فِيهِ وَساءَ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ حِمْلاً (101) abiding in it. How evil a burden will it be for them on the Day of Resurrection! in the burden (في الوزر). (١٠٢) يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ ** (102) The day the Trumpet is blown, and We gather the guilty on that day, blue-eyed, And it was read (قُرِئَ) as نُفِخَ with the ن [instead of يُنْفَخُ]. وَنَحْشُرُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ ** And it was read (قُرِئَ) as يُحْشَرُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ زُرْقاً. It was said: it means blue-eyed (زُرْق الْعُيُون), because blueness (الزّرقة) is the worst and most hated color of the eye among the Arabs. And it was said: meaning blind, for the pupil of the blind person becomes blue (تَزْرَاق). And it was said: thirsty (عِطَاشًا), such that blueness (الزّرقة) appears in their eyes. And al-Qummi said: their eyes will be blue (مُزْرَقَّة) and they will not be able to blink them (يَطْرِفُوهَا).

(١٠٣) يَتَخافَتُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (They will murmur among themselves) They lower their voices due to what fills their chests of terror and dread. إِنْ لَبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا عَشْراً (You did not tarry but ten [days]) They deem the duration of their stay in the world or in the grave brief because of its vanishing. (١٠٤) نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَقُولُونَ ** (We know best what they say) And it is the duration of their tarrying. إِذْ يَقُولُ أَمْثَلُهُمْ طَرِيقَةً ** (when the most upright among them in conduct says) The most just among them. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: the most knowledgeable among them and the most righteous among them. إِنْ لَبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا يَوْماً** (You did not tarry but a day)

footnote (١) ذرّت الريح الشيء ذرواً : أطارته وأذهبته. (1) The wind scattered (ذرّت) the thing (الشيء) with a scattering (ذرواً): it blew it away (أطارته) and took it away (وأذهبته).

(١٠٥) وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْجِبالِ ** (105) And they ask you about the mountains. — about the state of their affair (مال أمرها). فَقُلْ يَنْسِفُها رَبِّي نَسْفاً ** Then say, 'My Lord will scatter them as a scattering, — He makes them like sand, then He sends the winds upon them, so they are dispersed.

In al-Majma' (المجمع): A man from Thaqif (ثقيف) asked the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم how the mountains, despite their magnitude, would be on the Day of Resurrection. He said: "Indeed, Allah drives them by making them like sand, then He sends the winds upon them, so they disperse them."

(١٠٦) **فَيَذَرُها ** (106) Then He leaves it

Then He leaves its resting places (مقارّها) or the earth. And its implication (the implication of the object pronoun ها) without [explicit] mention is due to the mountains indicating it, like His saying: ما تَرَكَ عَلَيْها مِنْ دَابَّةٍ ** (He has not left upon it any creature) قاعاً (a قاع), empty, صَفْصَفاً (a صفصف), level, such that its parts are on a single level (صفّ واحد). Al-Qummi (said): The قاع is that which has no soil (تراب) in it, and the صفصف is that which has no vegetation (نبات) for it.

(١٠٧) **لا تَرى فِيها عِوَجاً وَلا أَمْتاً ** (107) you will not see in it any crookedness or unevenness.
