[Meaning] crookedness (اعوجاجاً) nor protuberance (نتوّاً). Al-Qummi said: الأمت (unevenness) is the elevation (الارتفاع), and العوج (crookedness) is the roughness (الحزون) and the mounds (الركوات). The three states (الأحوال الثلاثة) are ranked before [this description]. The first two are based on perception (الاحساس), and the third is based on measurement (المقياس). And for that reason, العوج (crookedness) is mentioned with the kasrah (vowel sound 'i'), and it specifically relates to meanings (المعاني).

(١٠٨) يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَّبِعُونَ الدَّاعِيَ ** (108) On that day they will follow the Caller (They will follow) the Caller (داعي الله) to the Gathering (المحشر). It is said that He is Israfil (إسرافيل), calling the people while standing upon the Rock of Jerusalem (صخرة بيت المقدس). They will approach Him from every direction (أوب) towards His side (صوبه). لا عِوَجَ لَهُ ** in whom there is no crookedness. No one called will turn crookedly away from Him (لا يعوّج له مدعوّ) nor deviate from Him (ولا يعدل عنه). وَخَشَعَتِ الْأَصْواتُ لِلرَّحْمنِ ** The voices will be hushed before the All-beneficent, And they will be lowered due to His awe (مهابته). فَلا تَسْمَعُ إِلَّا هَمْساً ** so you will hear nothing but a murmur. A hidden sound (صوتاً خفيّاً).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will gather the people on a single plain (صعيد واحد), barefoot and naked. They will be made to stand in the Gathering (المحشر) until they sweat profusely and their breathing becomes labored. They will remain in that state for the duration of fifty years. This is the meaning of the Almighty's saying: **وَخَشَعَتِ الْأَصْواتُ لِلرَّحْمنِ فَلا تَسْمَعُ إِلَّا هَمْساً ** (The voices will be hushed before the All-beneficent, so you will hear nothing but a murmur.)

He (peace be upon him) said: Then a caller (مناد) will call out from the direction of the Throne (تلقاء العرش): "Where is the Unlettered Prophet (النبيّ الاميّ)?" The people will say: "You have made us hear, so name Him by His name." Then He will call out: "Where is the Prophet of Mercy (نبيّ الرّحمة)? Where is Muhammad ibn Abdullah, the Unlettered (محمّد بن عبد الله الأميّ)?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم will then advance before all the people until He reaches a Pool (حوض) whose length is the distance between Aylah (ايلة) and San'a (صنعا). He will stand upon it, and then He will call out for your Companion (صاحبكم). Then Ali (peace be upon him) will advance before the people and stand with Him. Then permission will be granted for the people, and they will pass. On that Day, some will approach the Pool (وارد الحوض) and some will be turned away from it (مصروف عنه).

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sees those who are turned away from the Pool among Our lovers (محبّينا), He will weep and say: "O Lord! The Shi'ah (شيعة) of Ali! I see them being turned away towards the Companions of the Fire (أصحاب النّار) and prevented from approaching the Pool (ورود الحوض)." He (peace be upon him) said: Then Allah will send an angel to Him, who will say to Him: "What makes You weep, O Muhammad?" He will reply: "It is for the people among the Shi'ah of Ali." The angel will say to Him: "Indeed, Allah says to You, O Muhammad: 'I have granted the Shi'ah of Ali to You, O Muhammad, and I have forgiven their sins for their love of You and Your progeny (عترتك). I have joined them with You and with those whom they affirmed (يقولون به), and We have placed them in Your company (زمرتك). So, let them approach Your Pool (حوضك).'"

Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: How many men and women will be weeping on that Day, calling out: "O Muhammad!" when they witness that! And no one who takes us as guardians (يتولّانا), loves us, disavows our enemies (عدوّنا), and hates them will remain on that Day except that they will be in Our party (حزبنا), with Us, and will approach Our Pool (حوضنا).

**(١٠٩) يَوْمَئِذٍ لا تَنْفَعُ الشَّفاعَةُ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمَنُ وَرَضِيَ لَهُ قَوْلاً ** (On that day intercession will not avail, except from him whom the All-beneficent permits, and whose word He approves.) Except the intercession of one whom He permitted and with whom He was pleased due to his status with Allah, or except one whom He permitted that intercession be made for him, and He was pleased, for his sake, with the word of the intercessor concerning his matter, or [He was pleased with] his word for his sake and concerning his matter.

يَعْلَمُ ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (110) He knows what is before them the states that preceded them وَما خَلْفَهُمْ ** and what is behind them and what comes after them, which they will encounter. Al-Qummi said: ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (what is before them) is what has passed concerning the reports of the Prophets (الأنبياء), وَما خَلْفَهُمْ ** and what is behind them is concerning the reports of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). وَلا يُحِيطُونَ بِهِ عِلْماً and they do not comprehend Him in knowledge.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: The creatures (الخلائق) do not comprehend Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in knowledge, since He, may He be blessed and exalted, has placed a covering upon the eyes of the hearts (أبصار القلوب). Thus, no intellect (فهم) can reach Him through modality (بالكيف), nor can any heart (قلب) affirm Him through definition (بالحدّ). Therefore, do not describe Him except as He has described Himself: لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ الْأَوَّلُ وَالْآخِرُ وَالظَّاهِرُ وَالْباطِنُ الْخالِقُ الْبارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ ** There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing, (3) the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden, the Creator, the Maker, the Former.** He created things, so there is nothing among the things that resembles Him, may He be blessed and exalted.

(١١١) وَعَنَتِ الْوُجُوهُ لِلْحَيِّ الْقَيُّومِ (111) And [all] faces shall be humbled before the Living, the Self-subsistent.

The faces shall be humbled and submit to Him, the submission of the captives (الْعُنَاة)—who are the prisoners in the hand of the All-Dominant King. وَقَدْ خابَ مَنْ حَمَلَ ظُلْماً (And disappointed shall be he who bears [the burden of] iniquity.)

(١١٢) وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحاتِ بعض الطاعات وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلا يَخافُ ظُلْماً منع ثواب مستحق بالوعد **وَلا هَضْماً ** ولا كسراً منه بنقصان. (112) But whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer, he will fear neither injustice nor diminution.

Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir عليه السلام: "And nothing from his deed will be diminished. As for ẓulman (ظلماً), He says it will not be taken away."

(١١٣) وَكَذلِكَ أَنْزَلْناهُ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا كلّه على هذه الوتيرة (113) And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran, all of it upon this manner, وَصَرَّفْنا فِيهِ مِنَ الْوَعِيدِ ** (and We have elaborated in it the warnings) repeating within it the verses of warning, لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ المعاصي so that they may guard against sins, and thus piety (التّقوى) becomes a disposition (ملكة) for them, أَوْ يُحْدِثُ لَهُمْ ذِكْراً ** (or it may bring about a reminder for them) [meaning] an admonition (عظة) and a lesson (اعتباراً) when they hear it, so that it restrains them from them [the sins]. And for this subtle point (النكتة), He attributed the guarding/piety (التقوى) to them and the bringing about (الأحداث) to the Quran.

(114) So exalted is Allah in His Essence (ذاته) and His Attributes (صفاته) above resembling the creatures (المخلوقين). الْمَلِكُ الْحَقُ ** (The Sovereign, the Real) He Whose command and prohibition are executed by right (بالاستحقاق). وَلا تَعْجَلْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يُقْضى إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُ (And do not be hasty with the Qur’ān before its revelation is concluded for you) Al-Qummi said: When the Quran was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he would hasten to recite it before the completion of the descent of the verse. So Allah revealed: وَلا تَعْجَلْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يُقْضى إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُ (And do not be hasty with the Qur’ān before its revelation is concluded for you), meaning: before He finishes reciting it. وَقُلْ رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْماً ** (and say, 'My Lord, increase me in knowledge.') Meaning: Ask Allah for an increase in knowledge instead of hastening, for what has been revealed to you, you shall certainly attain it. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "If a day comes upon me in which I do not increase in knowledge that brings me closer to Allah, then may Allah not bless me in the rising of its sun." In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام was asked, "Who is the most knowledgeable of people?" He said: "He who gathers the knowledge of the people to his own knowledge." And narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام), from his forefathers عليهم السلام, from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "The superiority of knowledge is more beloved to Allah than the superiority of worship."

(١١٥) وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ (115) And certainly We made a covenant with Adam earlier, We commanded him. It is said: The angel (الملك) advanced toward him, and He entrusted him, and He resolved upon him, and He made a covenant with him when He commanded him. فَنَسِيَ ** then he forgot the covenant and did not pay attention to it. وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً ** and We did not find any resolve in him. (meaning: firmness of opinion and steadfastness upon the command).

Al-Qummi said: [This refers to] what He forbade him from, which was eating from the tree.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah the Almighty made a covenant with Adam that he should not approach this tree. When the time came which was in Allah's knowledge that he would eat from it, he forgot, so he ate from it. And this is the Almighty's saying: And certainly We made a covenant (the Ayah).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Indeed, Allah said to Adam and his wife: "Do not approach it," meaning: "Do not eat from it." So they said: "Yes, O our Lord, we will not approach it nor eat from it." But they did not make an exception (يستثنيا) in their saying "Yes." So Allah entrusted them in that matter to themselves and to their remembrance (ذكرهما).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Man (الإنسان) was named insan because he forgets (ينسى). Allah said: And certainly We made a covenant with Adam earlier, then he forgot.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of them (peace be upon them): He was asked how Allah took Adam by forgetfulness (بالنسيان). He replied: "Indeed, he did not forget. And how could he forget when he was remembering it, and Iblis was saying to him: ما نَهاكُما رَبُّكُما عَنْ هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةِ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونا مَلَكَيْنِ أَوْ تَكُونا مِنَ الْخالِدِينَ (20) Your Lord did not forbid you from this tree except lest you should become angels or become immortal.

I say: Perhaps what was forgotten was the firm resolve (عزيمة) of the prohibition, such that it would not accept interpretation (التّأويل) or dispensation (الرخصة). And what was not forgotten was the origin (أصل) of the prohibition. Or it is said: What was forgotten was the affirmation (الإقرار) of the virtue (فضيلة) of the Prophet and the Successor (الوصيّ) and their infallible descendants (peace be upon them). And the forgetfulness (النسيان) here is in the meaning of abandonment (التّرك), as indicated by other narrations (الاخبار الآخر).

So, in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said regarding the Almighty's saying: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ (And certainly We made a covenant with Adam earlier), [it refers to] words concerning Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and the Imams from their descendants, فَنَسِيَ (then he forgot). By Allah, thus was it revealed to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. And therein [it is narrated].

And in al-Ilal and al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: He made a covenant with him concerning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imams (peace be upon them) after Him, but he abandoned [it] (فترك) and did not have resolve (عزم) regarding them, that they are thus. And indeed, the possessors of resolve (أولو العزم) were named so because He made a covenant with them concerning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and the Successors (الاوصياء) after Him, and the Mahdi and His conduct (سيرته). So their resolve (عزمهم) was unanimous that this was so, and they affirmed it (الإقرار به).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: And the covenant (الميثاق) was taken from the possessors of resolve (أولي العزم): "Indeed, I am your Lord, and Muhammad is My Messenger, and Ali is the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and his Successors (اوصيائه) after him are the guardians of My command (وُلاة أمري) and the treasurers of My knowledge (خزّان علمي), and indeed, the Mahdi (peace be upon him) is the one through Whom I will grant victory to My religion, and through Whom I will manifest My dominion (دولتي), and through Whom I will take vengeance upon My enemies, and through Whom I will be worshipped willingly and unwillingly (طوعاً وكرهاً)." They said: "We affirm, O Lord, and we bear witness." But Adam did not deny nor did he affirm. Thus, the resolve (العزيمة) was established for these five concerning the Mahdi (may Allah hasten his reappearance), and Adam did not have resolve (عزم) to affirm Him. And this is the Almighty's saying: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَنَسِيَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً (And certainly We made a covenant with Adam earlier, then he forgot, and We did not find any resolve in him). He said: Indeed, it is [read as] "then he abandoned (فترك)."

(١١٦) وَإِذْ قُلْنا لِلْمَلائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ أَبى (116) And when We said to the angels, 'Prostrate before Adam,' they prostrated, but not Iblis: he refused. The discussion concerning this has already preceded.

فَقُلْنا يا آدَمُ إِنَّ هذا عَدُوٌّ لَكَ وَلِزَوْجِكَ فَلا يُخْرِجَنَّكُما مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ فَتَشْقى (١١٧) (117) We said, 'O Adam, this is indeed an enemy to you and to your wife. So do not let him expel you both from paradise, or you will be miserable.'

It was said: He singled him out by attributing the misery (الشقاء) to him after they both shared in the expulsion, by way of sufficiency, as his misery necessarily entails her misery, given that he is the maintainer (قيّم) over her, and [also] to preserve the verse endings (الفواصل). Or because the intended meaning of the misery (الشقاء) is the toil (التعب) in seeking livelihood (طلب المعاش), and that is the duty (وظيفة) of men, and what follows supports this.
(١١٨) إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيها وَلا تَعْرى (118) It is indeed [a promise] for you that you will not be hungry therein, nor naked,
** (١١٩) وَأَنَّكَ لا تَظْمَؤُا فِيها وَلا تَضْحى (119) and that you will not thirst therein, nor suffer from the sun.

(١٢٠) Then Satan whispered to him, and he conveyed his whispering to him. قالَ يا آدَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلى شَجَرَةِ الْخُلْدِ ** (He said, 'O Adam, shall I show you the tree of immortality?) The tree, whoever eats from it will live forever and will never die at all. وَمُلْكٍ لا يَبْلى** (and a kingdom that does not decay?') It does not vanish and does not weaken.

(١٢١) فَأَكَلا مِنْها فَبَدَتْ لَهُما سَوْآتُهُما وَطَفِقا يَخْصِفانِ عَلَيْهِما مِنْ وَرَقِ الْجَنَّةِ (121) So they both ate of it, and their shameful parts became manifest to them, and they began to stitch over themselves with the leaves of paradise. They began sticking the leaves onto their shameful parts for concealment. وَعَصى آدَمُ رَبَّهُ ** by eating from the tree فَغَوى ** He deviated from what was required and failed, since he sought eternity by eating it.

(122) Then his Lord chose him, selected him, and brought him near by urging him towards repentance and granting him success (التّوفيق) for it. فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ (He turned to him clemently) So He accepted his repentance when he repented, وَهَدى (and guided him) towards steadfastness upon repentance and clinging to the means of infallibility (العصمة).

قالَ اهْبِطا مِنْها جَمِيعاً بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ (123) He said, 'Get down from it, both of you, all together, as enemies of one another.'

The address is primarily for Adam and Eve, and for Iblis. And since they [Adam and Eve] were the origin of the progeny, He addressed them [in the dual form], and thus He addressed them [the progeny, in the plural form]. The entirety of this story and the exegesis of these verses have already passed in Sūrat al-Baqarah.

فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ مِنِّي هُدىً ** (Then, should any guidance come to you from Me) a Book (كتاب) and a Messenger (رسول).

فَمَنِ اتَّبَعَ هُدايَ فَلا يَضِلُ ** (whoever follows My guidance will not go astray) in this world (الدنيا).

وَلا يَشْقى ** (nor will he be miserable.) in the Hereafter (الآخرة).

In al-Kafi, narrated anonymously (مضمراً): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "[It refers to] the one who affirms the Imams (الائمّة), follows their command, and does not deem their obedience permissible [to reject]."

(١٢٤) وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْ ذِكْرِي فَإِنَّ لَهُ مَعِيشَةً ضَنْكاً ضيّقاً وَنَحْشُرُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَعْمى (124) But whoever disregards My remembrance—his shall be a wretched life, constricted (ضيّقاً), and We shall raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.
قالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِي أَعْمى (١٢٥) وَقَدْ كُنْتُ بَصِيراً. (125) He will say, 'My Lord! Why have You raised me blind, while I was sighted?'

He said: "Thus," meaning: "I did the like of that." Then He explained it: "Our signs came to you, clear and luminous, but you forgot them," meaning you became blind to them and abandoned them, not looking at them. "And thus," meaning, just like your abandonment of them, الْيَوْمَ تُنْسى ** (today you will be forgotten), meaning you will be abandoned in blindness and torment.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: فَإِنَّ لَهُ مَعِيشَةً ضَنْكاً ** (Indeed, there will be a hard life for him) He said: "By Allah, this is for the Nussab (النصاب)." He was asked: "We saw them throughout their long lives in sufficiency until they died." He said: "By Allah, that is in the Return (الرّجعة); they will eat filth (العذرة)."

And in al-Kafi, regarding the Almighty's saying: مَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْ ذِكْرِي ** (Whoever disregards My remembrance) He said: "The guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." أَعْمى (blind). He said: "Meaning, blind of sight in the Hereafter, and blind of heart in this world concerning the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And he will be bewildered on the Day of Resurrection, saying: لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِي (Why have You raised me up) [the rest of] the verse." He said: "The signs (الآيات) are the Imams (peace be upon them). فَنَسِيتَها ** (and you forgot them) means you abandoned them. وَكَذلِكَ الْيَوْمَ (and thus today) you will be abandoned in the Fire, just as you abandoned the Imams, for you did not obey their command and did not listen to their saying."

And in al-Faqih, al-Majma', and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who never performed Hajj (الحج) despite having wealth. He said: "He is one of those about whom Allah said: وَنَحْشُرُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَعْمى ** (and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of Resurrection) It was said: "Glory be to Allah! Blind?" He replied: "Allah blinded him from the path of goodness (طريق الخير)."

And al-Qummi narrated it concerning the path to Paradise (طريق الجنّة). And in al-Kafi, there is that which is close to it.

وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي مَنْ أَسْرَفَ وَلَمْ يُؤْمِنْ بِآياتِ رَبِّهِ (127) Thus do We requite him who is extravagant and does not believe in the signs of his Lord.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means whoever associates others with the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَلَمْ يُؤْمِنْ بِآياتِ رَبِّهِ ** (and does not believe in the signs of his Lord) He abandoned the Imams (الأئمّة) out of obstinacy, so he did not follow their traces (اثارهم) and did not take them as guardians (يتولّهم). وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبْقى ** (and the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more lasting) than the hardship of life (ضنك العيش) and than the blindness (العمى).

(١٢٨) أَفَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ (128) Has it not guided them

Al-Qummi says, explaining to them: كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ (How many generations We destroyed before them) — Our destruction of them — يَمْشُونَ فِي مَساكِنِهِمْ (They walk in their dwellings) and they witness the traces of their destruction. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِأُولِي النُّهى (Indeed, in that are signs for those who possess intellect) — for those with intellects (العقول) that restrain (النّاهية) them from heedlessness (التغافل) and willful blindness (التعامي).

(١٢٩) وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ (129) And were it not for a word that went before from your Lord which is the promise to delay the punishment of this nation until the Hereafter, لَكانَ لِزاماً ** it would have been inevitable the like of what descended upon 'Ad and Thamud would have been binding upon these disbelievers. وَأَجَلٌ مُسَمًّى ** and a specified term It is conjoined (عطف) with kalimah (كلمة), meaning: And were it not for the promise to delay the punishment and a specified term (أجل مسمّى) for their lifespans or for their punishment, the punishment would have been inevitable (لزاماً). And the separation (الفصل) [between the clauses] is to indicate that each one independently negates the inevitability (لزوم) of the punishment. Al-Qummi said: Al-Lizam (اللّزام) is destruction (الهلاك). He said: The punishment would have descended upon them, but He has delayed them until a specified term (أجل مسمّى).

(١٣٠) فَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ غُرُوبِها وَمِنْ آناءِ اللَّيْلِ (130) So be patient with what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the sunrise and before its setting, and during the watches of the night.

And from its hours (ساعاته). It is the plural of إنا (with kasrah and qasr) and أنَاء (with fath and madd).

**فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرافَ النَّهارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضى ** (then glorify [Him] and in the watches of the day, so that you may be pleased.)

[The phrase لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضى means] hoping that you may attain from Allah that by which you please yourself. And it was recited with the passive construction (بالبناء على المفعول), meaning: your Lord will please you (يرضيك ربّك).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "It is an obligation (فريضة) upon every Muslim to say, before the sunrise and before its setting, ten times: لا اله الّا الله وَحده لا شريك له له الملك وله الحمد يحيي ويميت وهو حيّ لا يموت بيده الخير وهو على كلّ شيء قدير (There is no god except Allah, alone, without any partner; to Him belongs the dominion, and to Him belongs the praise; He gives life and causes death, and He is the Ever-Living Who never dies; in His hand is the good, and He has power over everything)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), regarding His saying: وَأَطْرافَ النَّهارِ ** (and in the watches of the day). He said: "It means voluntary prayer (تطوّع) during the day."

(١٣١) وَلا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ that your gaze extends إِلى ما مَتَّعْنا بِهِ to what We have provided therewith, admiring it and wishing that you had the like of it, أَزْواجاً مِنْهُمْ meaning: types (أَصْنَافًا) of the disbelievers, زَهْرَةَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا meaning: its adornment and splendor, لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ meaning: so that We may try them and test them therein, or so that We may punish them in the Hereafter because of it. **وَرِزْقُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقى ** meaning: that is, the guidance and prophethood, which does not cease.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When this Ayah was revealed, the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) sat up straight, then He said: "Whoever does not seek solace (يَتَعَزَّ) through the solace (عَزَاء) of Allah, his soul will be torn apart by regrets over the world. And whoever follows with his gaze what is in the hands of people, his worry will be prolonged, and his anger will not be cured. And whoever does not recognize that Allah has bestowed a favor upon him except in food and drink, his lifespan will be shortened, and his punishment will draw near."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), He said: "Beware lest your soul aspires to those above you. Sufficient is what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): فَلا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُهُمْ ** (So let not their wealth nor their children impress you) And He said: لا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ** (Do not extend your eyes) [referring to the rest of the Ayah]."

(١٣٢) وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِالصَّلاةِ وَاصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْها (132) And bid your family to observe the prayer, and be constant therein. and be constant therein. لا نَسْئَلُكَ رِزْقاً ** (We do not ask you for any provision) that you provide for yourself or your family. نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُكَ ** (We provide for you) and for them. So, free your mind for the Hereafter. وَالْعاقِبَةُ ** (and the outcome) the praiseworthy (outcome) لِلتَّقْوى ** (is for God-fearing piety) for the possessor of piety (التّقوى).

In al-Awali and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Allah commanded His Prophet to single out his Household (أهل بيته) and his family (أهله) apart from the people, so that the people might know that his family holds a station with Allah that is not held by others. Thus, He commanded them generally along with the people, and then commanded them specifically.

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Allah distinguished us with this particular distinction (الخصوصيّة) when He commanded us, along with the Ummah (الأمّة), to establish the prayer, and then singled us out apart from the Ummah (الأمّة). Thus, after the revelation of this Ayah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would come to the door of Ali and Fatima (peace be upon them) for nine months, every day, at the time of every prayer, five times, saying: "The Prayer! May Allah have mercy upon you." And Allah has not honored anyone from the descendants (ذراري) of the Prophets with a grace (الكرامة) like this grace with which He honored us and singled us out from all their households (اهل بيتهم).

Al-Qummi added, in a mursal (مرسلاً) narration, and in al-Majma' from al-Khudri, after the saying "May Allah have mercy upon you": إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً (Allah desires only to keep away uncleanness from you, O People of the Household, and cleanse you thoroughly.) Al-Qummi (said): He did not cease doing that every day when He was present in Medina until He departed the world.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was diligent (نصباً) in prayer even after He was given the glad tidings of Paradise, due to the saying of Allah, the Glorified: وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ (And bid your family) بِالصَّلاةِ وَاصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْها (to observe the prayer, and be constant therein). So, He would command it and be patient with Himself regarding it. And in al-Kafi: the like of it.

وَقالُوا لَوْ لا يَأْتِينا بِآيَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ أَوَلَمْ تَأْتِهِمْ بَيِّنَةُ ما فِي الصُّحُفِ الْأُولى (133) (133) And they say, 'Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?' Has there not come to them the manifest proof of what is in the former scriptures?

which indicates his truthfulness in claiming prophethood. Has there not come to them the manifest proof of what is in the former scriptures (الصُّحُفِ الْأُولى), namely the Torah (التّوراة), the Gospel (الإنجيل), and the rest of the heavenly Books (الكتب السماوية)? For indeed, the inclusion of the Qur’an (القرآن) of the essence of what is in them—concerning doctrines (العقائد) and universal rulings (الأحكام الكلّية)—despite the fact that the one who brought it (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) had neither seen them nor learned from those who taught them, is a manifest miracle (اعجاز بيّن).

(١٣٤) وَلَوْ أَنَّا أَهْلَكْناهُمْ بِعَذابٍ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ لَقالُوا رَبَّنا لَوْ لا أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنا رَسُولاً فَنَتَّبِعَ آياتِكَ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ نَذِلَ وَنَخْزى (١٣٤) (134) And had We destroyed them with a punishment before it, they would have surely said, 'Our Lord, why did You not send us an apostle, so that we might follow Your signs before we were humiliated and disgraced?'

**وَلَوْ أَنَّا أَهْلَكْناهُمْ بِعَذابٍ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ**
— meaning: before Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله — **لَقالُوا رَبَّنا لَوْ لا أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنا رَسُولاً فَنَتَّبِعَ آياتِكَ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ نَذِلَ**
— meaning: by killing (*بالقتل*) and captivity (*والسّبي*) in this world (*في الدّنيا*) — **وَنَخْزى**
— meaning: by entering the Fire (*بدخول النّار*) in the Hereafter (*في الآخرة*).

(135) Say: Everyone is waiting (مُتَرَبِّصٌ), anticipating what his affair will lead to. فَتَرَبَّصُوا فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَصْحابُ الصِّراطِ السَّوِيِ (so wait! Soon you will know who are the people of the straight path) — the middle (الوسط) — وَمَنِ اهْتَدى (and who is guided) from error (الضّلالة).

قُلْ كُلٌّ مُتَرَبِّصٌ فَتَرَبَّصُوا فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَصْحابُ الصِّراطِ السَّوِيِ وَمَنِ اهْتَدى (20:135) (Say, 'Everyone is waiting; so wait! Soon you will know who are the people of the straight path, and who is guided.')

In Kashf al-Mahajjah, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), in a hadith: It was said: "And who is the guardian (الوليّ), O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Your guardian (وليّكم) in this time is I, and after me, my successor (وصيّي), and after my successor, for every time there are the proofs (حجج) of Allah, so that you do not say what the misguided (الضّلال) before you said when their prophet left them: رَبَّنا لَوْ لا أَرْسَلْتَ الآية (Our Lord, why did You not send... the Ayah)." And the completion of their misguidance was only due to their ignorance of the signs (الآيات), and they are the successors (الأوصياء). So Allah answered them: قُلْ كُلٌّ مُتَرَبِّصٌ الآية (Say: Everyone is waiting... the Ayah). And their waiting (تربّصهم) was only that they said: "We are at ease (في سعة) regarding the knowledge of the successors (الأوصياء) until the Imam of His knowledge (امام علمه) announces himself."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Do not abandon the recitation of Surah Taha, for Allah loves it and loves whoever recites it. And whoever persists in its recitation, Allah will give him his book [of deeds] in his right hand on the Day of Resurrection, and He will not hold him accountable for what he did in Islam. And He will grant him such a reward in the Hereafter until he is satisfied." May Allah grant us its recitation.
